t_ch: 听说最近下层区的治安越来越好了，没准哪天我会去那边转转。不过下层区人好像很粗鲁，应该不适合做我的朋友。
t_en: I heard the Underworld area is getting safer by the day, maybe I'll visit someday. However, the locals seem pretty rough around the edges, probably not the ideal friend material for me.

t_ch: 你说交朋友这个事情真挺神奇的，之前让你帮我物色了那么多人，没有一个愿意和我做朋友。
t_en: It's funny how friendship works. I had you scout out so many people, and yet no one wanted to be my friend.

t_ch: 结果就在我即将放弃的时候，认识了雷金，也许这就是缘分吧。
t_en: Just when I was about to give up, I met Regin, maybe it was meant to be.

t_ch: 你的科学期刊办的怎么样了？
t_en: How's your science journal doing?

t_ch: 四个字，热火朝天。创刊才短短几个月，我就收到了几百篇来自社会各界的科学论文。
t_en: In two words: On fire. Just a few months after launch, I've already received hundreds of scientific papers from across society.

t_ch: 虽然其中大多都是平庸之作，但还是有几篇内容令我非常感兴趣。比如《利用黑洞制作永动机的可能性》《关于水猴子物种存在的若干证据》……
t_en: While most are mediocre, a few articles caught my eye, like The Possibility of Creating a Perpetual Motion Machine Using Black Holes and Evidence Supporting the Existence of Water Monkey Species...

t_ch: 真是高手在民间啊。
t_en: It's true what they say, there are undiscovered geniuses among us.

t_ch: 没错！这正是我把期刊命名为《民间科学》的原因。长期以来，科学的解释权都被那些尸位素餐的官方学者牢牢掌握，导致贝洛伯格的科学水平严重落后。
t_en: That's precisely why I named it Citizen Science. For too long, the power to define science has been monopolized by complacent academics, holding back Belobog's scientific advancement.

t_ch: 不过科学的春天已经到了，在我的带领下，贝洛伯格马上就能迎来技术爆炸。
t_en: But the renaissance of science is upon us. Under my leadership, Belobog is on the verge of a technological revolution.

t_ch: 感觉像是伪科学合集。
t_en: Sounds like a compendium of pseudoscience, doesn't it?

t_ch: 我非常理解你的看法，因为你是个普通人，缺乏对先进理论的洞察。
t_en: I understand where you're coming from because you're a normal person without insight into advanced theories.

t_ch: 我这么说吧，许多现在全宇宙公认的真理，在几个琥珀纪之前都是伪科学。
t_en: Let me put it this way, many truths now universally acknowledged across the universe were considered pseudoscience just a few Amber Eras ago.

t_ch: 华劳斯的投资算是打水漂了。
t_en: Wallace's investment has gone down the drain.

t_ch: 华劳斯可不像你这样目光短浅，虽然他不懂具体的理论细节，但是他有尊重科学的精神，以及…很多的钱。
t_en: Unlike you, Wallace isn't shortsighted. Even though he may not grasp all the detailed theories, he respects the spirit of scientific inquiry, and... well, he also has deep pockets.

t_ch: 总之，科学研究是超长周期投资，我相信他有足够的耐心。
t_en: Anyway, scientific research is a long-term investment, and I believe he has the patience for it.

t_ch: 你走南闯北的应该见过不少世面，要是在旅行途中有什么新奇发现，欢迎给《民间科学》投稿。
t_en: Given your extensive travels, if you ever come across anything intriguing, feel free to contribute to Citizen Science.

t_ch: 至于稿费嘛，绝对亏待不了你。
t_en: And don't worry about the fee, we won't shortchange you.

t_ch: 来聊聊天吧…
t_en: Let's have a chat...

t_ch: 好啊，正好我手头没什么事。
t_en: No problem. I just so happen to be free right now.

t_ch: 假期过得怎么样？
t_en: How's your holiday been?

t_ch: 头两天有点不适应，天还没亮就起床了，还以为自己在前线，下意识的要去检查物资，结果被我老婆一顿笑话……
t_en: The first few days were tough. I kept waking up before dawn, as if I was still on the front lines, automatically checking supplies, only to be laughed at by my wife...

t_ch: 不过家里的生活真是惬意啊，有可口的饭菜，温暖的地髓炉，相亲相爱的家人。即使一天什么都不做，关是躺在椅子上看报纸就很好。
t_en: Life at home is truly blissful, complete with delicious meals, the warmth of a Geomarrow furnace, and loving family members. Even just lounging in a chair and reading the newspaper all day feels perfect.

t_ch: 我现在已经盼着下次休假了，为了这一天早点到来，我必须更加认真地做好本职工作。
t_en: I'm eagerly anticipating my next vacation, and to make it arrive sooner, I'm committed to working harder at my job.

t_ch: 你接着忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 手头确实还有活没干完，那我接着维护军备了。
t_en: There's still work to be done. I'll get back to maintaining the armaments.

t_ch: 你爸爸已经回前线了？
t_en: Has your dad gone back to the front lines?

t_ch: 唉…是啊，假期过得好快，感觉还没和老爸相处多久呢。
t_en: Ah... yes, the holiday went by too quickly. I feel like I barely spent any time with him.

t_ch: 要不你去前线探望他？
t_en: Why don't you visit him on the front lines?

t_ch: 我早就有这个想法了，但是老爸说前线非常危险，我一个小女孩不要随便过去。
t_en: I've thought about it, but my dad says it's dangerous and not a place for a girl like me.

t_ch: 哼，我明明都已经这么大了，早就不是小女孩了。
t_en: Hmph, I'm not a kid anymore. I've grown up.

t_ch: 那里是很危险。
t_en: It is dangerous, truly.

t_ch: 在爸爸眼里你始终是小女孩。
t_en: In your father's eyes, you'll always be his little girl.

t_ch: 好吧，既然你都这么说了，那我还是老老实实等老爸下次休假吧。
t_en: Well, since you put it that way, I guess I'll just wait patiently for his next break.

t_ch: 他应该很快就能退役了。
t_en: He should be retiring soon.

t_ch: 话是这么说，但是他都已经延期退役好几次了，每次都说部队离不开他，不知道的还以为他是将军呢。
t_en: He keeps postponing his retirement, saying the unit can't function without him. Honestly, you'd think he was a general or something.

t_ch: 其实我也知道老爸当了几十年的兵，对部队有很深的感情，但是我和妈妈也很想念他啊。
t_en: I understand that Dad has been in the military for decades and has a strong bond with it, but Mom and I really miss him too.

t_ch: 希望这次他是真的能退役吧，他的年纪真经不起折腾了。
t_en: I hope this time he can retire, he's not getting any younger.

t_ch: 如果你下次还要去前线，麻烦帮我给老爸带句话，让他安安心心工作，我会把妈妈照顾好，等他平安归来的。
t_en: If you go to the front lines next time, please tell my dad to work peacefully and not worry, as I'll take good care of Mom and wait for his safe return.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 咦，你又来听小女子开嗓啊，还是说有什么其他的事吗？
t_en: Hey, are you here to hear this young lady sing again, or is there something else?

t_ch: 你要登台了吗？
t_en: Are you about to go on stage?

t_ch: 哎呀呀～说起来还是托塔米拉小姐的福呢。小女子先前虽有担任过一部新剧的女主角，可惜剧评人说这种题材太俗套了……
t_en: Oh, I have Miss Tamila to thank for this. I once starred as the lead in a new play, but the critics brushed it off as being too cliché.

t_ch: 好在小女子现在是塔米拉小姐的好朋友～她为我介绍了一位新锐导演，小女子运气真好～被他看中，留在他的新剧里担任女主角呢。
t_en: Thankfully, Miss Tamila and I are close friends now. She introduced me to an up-and-coming director, and, lucky me, he cast me as the lead in his next production.

t_ch: 你跟塔米拉还有联系吗？
t_en: Are you still in touch with Tamila?

t_ch: 那当然，塔米拉小姐对小女子的恩情，小女子这辈子都不会忘的。
t_en: Absolutely, I'll always be grateful to Miss Tamila for her kindness.

t_ch: 小女子每天都会给她发消息，不过塔米拉小姐比较忙，她最近好像都待在录音室里，有时候回消息会比较慢。
t_en: I message her every day to keep in touch, but Miss Tamila has been swamped lately, spending most of her time in the recording studio. Sometimes it takes her a while to reply.

t_ch: 音乐公司那边很看重她，说她这低沉的嗓音是什么「烟嗓」，是唱爵士的好苗子～虽然小女子还没听过这种风格的音乐，但还是由衷地为塔米拉小姐感到高兴。
t_en: The music company holds her in high regard, calling her deep voice a "smoky tone" that's perfect for jazz. I haven't experienced that style of music myself, but I'm genuinely thrilled for her success.

t_ch: 塔米拉怎么不在这儿了？
t_en: Why isn't Tamila here?

t_ch: 哎呀呀～之前不是跟你说过了吗～塔米拉小姐被「公司」的经纪人看中，现在离开贝洛伯格去外面发展了。
t_en: Oh, didn't I tell you? Miss Tamila was discovered by an agent from the IPC and has left Belobog to further her career.

t_ch: 小女子早就觉得，黄金歌剧院配不上塔米拉小姐，她值得有更广阔更璀璨的未来。
t_en: I've long believed that the Golden Theater isn't worthy of Miss Tamila. She's meant for a future that's far more expansive and dazzling.

t_ch: 你跟塔米拉关系好吗？
t_en: Are you close with Tamila?

t_ch: 你这问题，叫小女子好生为难。这叫什么话？塔米拉小姐帮了我这么多，她让小女子不再成为她的影子，还为我找到了走到台前的路。
t_en: That's a weird question to ask. Miss Tamila has done so much for me — she helped me step out of her shadow and showed me the way to the spotlight.

t_ch: 小女子恨不得照顾塔米拉小姐一辈子，这样才能表达出小女子对她的感谢呢。你这问题问得到倒像是小女子和塔米拉小姐有仇，这也太奇怪了吧？
t_en: I'd gladly stick by her side for life if it meant showing her how grateful I am. Honestly, the way you put it makes it sound like I have something against her, which is just ridiculous.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 没事呀～小女子正好可以继续练歌了～
t_en: No worries, I was just about to continue practicing my singing.

t_ch: 来碗「海的味道」。
t_en: Would you like a bowl of "Taste of the Sea"?

t_ch: 客官真不好意思，这「海的味道」已经下架了，因为核心材料——波月古海的水弄不到了。
t_en: I'm sorry, guest, "Taste of the Sea" is no longer available as the core ingredient — water from the Lunarescent Depths can't be obtained anymore.

t_ch: 您也知道波月古海是持明族的圣地，之前虽然也经常有持明族在海边巡逻，但总能被我找到空档打两桶水。
t_en: The Lunarescent Depths is a sacred site for the Vidyadhara. Though they used to patrol the shoreline, I was always able to sneak in and scoop up a couple of barrels of water.

t_ch: 结果现在他们弄了几十只机巧鸟，一天二十四小时在岸边巡逻，见着行为可疑的人就报警，一点机会也不给啊……
t_en: But now they've deployed dozens of cycranes to constantly patrol the shore, sounding the alarm at any hint of suspicious activity — leaving no opportunity for us.

t_ch: 不过本店还有其他茶品，味道也不错，您可以尝尝。
t_en: We've still got plenty of other delightful teas in the shop — why not have a try?

t_ch: 这波月古海清净了不少…
t_en: The Lunarescent Depths have become significantly more tranquil.

t_ch: 自从有了机巧鸟帮忙巡逻以后，那些放生的人就不敢来了。
t_en: With the cycranes now on patrol, those who used to release animals into the wild there have stopped coming.

t_ch: 不过这样也有问题，波月古海毕竟是传播持明族文化的重地，很多正常参观旅游的人也被赶走了。
t_en: That said, it's created another issue. Lunarescent Depths is a key site for Vidyadhara cultural outreach, and now even regular visitors and tourists are being turned away.

t_ch: 回头得升级下机巧鸟的识别算法，让它能精准识别出潜在的破坏分子。
t_en: We need to upgrade the recognition algorithms of the cycranes to accurately identify potential disruptors.

t_ch: 雅努萨波利斯，不愧是翁法罗斯的中心。能来到这里，老夫的人生也算是圆满了呐。
t_en: Janusopolis... It's really what you'd expect from the heart of Amphoreus. Being able to make it here, I feel like my life has been worth something.

t_ch: 风景确实壮丽。
t_en: The view really is beautiful.

t_ch: 是啊。不过，对老夫来说，来到这雅努斯之城的意义，也的确比平常人更多一层……
t_en: It sure is. Although, my visit here has far more meaning than the average man's.

t_ch: 这也就是个普通景点。
t_en: The view looks pretty average to me?

t_ch: 哈哈，年轻人你这么说也没错。雅努斯之城虽然崇高，但也是充满人间烟火的一座城市，比起奥赫玛或是哀地里亚来没有本质区别。只不过，对老夫来说……
t_en: Haha, you're not wrong, young one. Though the city of Janus is majestic, it is also a place where people live their normal daily lives, no different to Okhema or Aidonia. However, in my eyes...

t_ch: …周游翁法罗斯之后终于抵达了它的中心，完成人生的最后一次朝圣，它的意义其实早就超过了圣城的风景究竟如何。
t_en: ...Now that I have traveled all of Amphoreus and arrived at its center, I have completed the final pilgrimage of my life. The meaning this place holds to me far exceeds the picturesque sights of the holy city.

t_ch: 据说在千年前的黄金世，三位灾厄泰坦尚未降临，那时的人们可以不太费力地抵达世界边界，在无尽之海中亲身体验{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}的奇迹。
t_en: Legend has it that during the Era Chrysea a thousand years ago, the three Calamity Titans had not yet descended on this world. Back then, people could travel to the ends of the earth with ease and personally witness the miracles of the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#} within the endless ocean.

t_ch: 可惜此后爆发了泰坦战争，又有黑潮作祟——人们无法去世界的尽头向{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}朝圣，便只能来到这座城市表达自己的崇敬之情。
t_en: Unfortunately, the Strife of the Titans broke out and the black tide was born... People were no longer able to make pilgrimages to the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} at the end of the earth. Their only way to express their reverence was to journey to this city.

t_ch: 老夫是个固执的人，想效法黄金世的古人走遍已知的整个世界…有幸众神垂怜，保我一路逢凶化吉。年轻人，你要是有兴趣，我可以和你讲讲古今朝圣的区别。
t_en: I am a stubborn old man, and I want to follow in the footsteps of my ancestors in the Era Chrysea and visit every corner of the known world... Fortunately, the Gods showed me mercy and protected me from harm along the way. Youngster, if you are interested, I can tell you the differences between the pilgrimages of today and ancient times.

t_ch: 年轻人，你要是有兴趣，我可以和你讲讲古今朝圣的区别。
t_en: Youngster, if you are interested, I can tell you the differences between the pilgrimages of today and ancient times.

t_ch: 好啊，让我学习学习。
t_en: Alright, I'm down to learn.

t_ch: 那太好了。但老夫毕竟不是祭司，也就能和你讲讲自己道听途说的一些东西。
t_en: Perfect. Although, I am no priest, all I can tell you is things I have learned along my travels.

t_ch: 黄金世的朝圣、雅努斯之城的意义、雅努斯之门的传说…嗯，有把握的大概就是这些吧。
t_en: The pilgrimage of the Era Chrysea, the significance of the City of Janus, the legend of the Gates of Janus... I'd say that's all I can really tell you about.

t_ch: 听听黄金世怎么朝圣。
t_en: Tell me about the pilgrimages of the Era Chrysea.

t_ch: 在遥远的黄金世，由于没有黑潮和战争，旅行非常安全，人们为了表达对泰坦的崇敬之情，往往会前往某位泰坦施行神迹的具体地点进行朝圣。
t_en: Back in the Era Chrysea, travelling was extremely safe as there was no black tide or war. In order to show their reverence to the Titans, people would often make pilgrimages to certain locations where Titans would perform miracles.

t_ch: 就拿这雅努萨波利斯来说吧，这里是{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}用万径之门的神力测算出的大地中心——从这里往任何方向走，抵达世界尽头的距离都是差不多的。
t_en: Let me take Janusopolis as an example: This is the center of the earth as defined by {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} using the divine power of the Gate of Infinity... If you travel in any direction from here, the distance to the end of the world is almost the same.

t_ch: 所以在黄金世，人们的朝圣旅途并不是从四面八方抵达这里：恰恰相反，他们会从这里出发，尽力抵达世界尽头，亲身穿过雅努斯在那里设立的门。
t_en: So, in the Era Chrysea, people would not conduct their pilgrimages from all directions to arrive here. On the contrary, they would start their pilgrimages from here and try their best to make it to the end of the world to personally pass through the gates that Janus themself had set there.

t_ch: 只可惜在如今的年代，人们身处黑潮包围之中，已经绝无可能再抵达那里…即使想效法古人，也只能象征性地周游世界，无法穿过黑潮去世界真正的尽头朝圣。
t_en: Unfortunately, in modern times, people are completely encircled by the black tide and can no longer make this journey... Even if they wish to do as the ancients did, they can only make a symbolic journey around the world. They cannot pass through the black tide to pilgrimage to the true end of the world.

t_ch: 听听雅努斯之城的意义。
t_en: Tell me about the significance of the City of Janus.

t_ch: 雅努斯之城不仅是{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}曾经垂迹的圣所，三位命运泰坦向世界发出神谕的中心…它还是整个翁法罗斯在地理上的中心。
t_en: The City of Janus is not only the holy land where {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} once left their mark, it also served as the center where the Fate Titans would declare prophecies to the world... It is the geographical center of all of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 在世界诞生之前，雅努斯就已经醒来：那时它落脚的混沌之地，就是如今雅努萨波利斯的前身。
t_en: Janus awakened before the world had even been born: The chaotic land where they stood back then was the predecessor of the Janusopolis that we know today.

t_ch: 正因为有雅努斯为世界开辟道路，支柱泰坦才能创造出天空、大地与海洋。
t_en: It was because Janus created a path for the world that the Pillar Titans were able to create the sky, earth, and sea.

t_ch: 所以，有一派祭司就认为，黄金世那些立于世界边缘的雅努斯之门，在最初就一并诞生于雅努萨波利斯这个世界的原点——
t_en: So, there is a faction of priests who believe that the Gates of Janus standing at the ends of the earth originated in Janusopolis, the starting point of this world.

t_ch: 是世界在造物世的诞生，是天空、大地、和海洋的膨胀，才把它们从这里推到了无比遥远的边境之地。
t_en: It was the birth of the world during the Era Creatura, the expansion of the skies, the earth, and the sea that pushed them to the distant borders where they now stand.

t_ch: 听听雅努斯之门的传说。
t_en: Tell me about the legend of the Gates of Janus

t_ch: 根据黄金世留下的记载，雅努斯之门是{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}建立在世界尽头的无数道门扉。
t_en: According to records left behind from the Era Chrysea, the Gates of Janus are a countless number of doors set up by {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} along the edge of the world.

t_ch: 如果你在无尽之海中找到了它们，那么只要穿过门扉，你就能奇迹般抵达世界的任何一处——
t_en: If you locate one in the Endless Seas, you need only to pass through it, and you can miraculously travel anywhere in the world instantly —

t_ch: 譬如回到这里，回到雅努萨波利斯。据说，通过了雅努斯之门后，你的船只会像奥赫玛的飞天坛递一样，拥有在空中飞行的能力。
t_en: You could return straight here, to Janusopolis, for instance. Legend has it that once you pass through the Gates of Janus, your ship will obtain the ability to soar through the skies like the Flying Amphora of Okhema.

t_ch: 只可惜如今世界的尽头早已被黑潮淹没…我们已经无法检验这种记载是真是假了。也许，它其实只是古人编出来的故事？
t_en: Unfortunately, the ends of the earth have now long since been devoured by the black tide... There is no way for us to test whether these records are true or not. Perhaps they are just stories that the ancients made up?

t_ch: 唉，可惜我们不是生活在黄金世啊。
t_en: Ah, what a pity that we do not live in the Era Chrysea.

t_ch: 没什么想听的了。
t_en: I think I'm good.

t_ch: 哈哈，我并不是祭司，很多东西也只是道听途说，讲得也不一定对。
t_en: Haha, I'm not a priest, so all I can tell you is what I have heard along my travels. There is no guarantee it is all true.

t_ch: 年轻人…人生就是一场朝圣，你只要无愧于自己的信仰就好。
t_en: Youngster... Life is a pilgrimage. You need only be devout to your beliefs.

t_ch: 虽然但是，我还有正事要办。
t_en: Nevertheless, I have things to do.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，年轻人确实还是忙一点好！
t_en: Hahaha, it is best for youngsters like yourself to keep busy!

t_ch: 人生就是一场朝圣…你只要无愧于自己的信仰，那，就是最大的虔诚。
t_en: Life is a pilgrimage. You need only be devout to your beliefs. That is the greatest form of piety.

t_ch: 嗯…原来如此，我明白了…无怪你的父亲如此忧虑深重。对于凡俗之人而言，雅努斯的神谕确实过于晦涩……
t_en: Hmm... I see. I understand... No wonder your father is so weighed down with concern. The prophecies of Janus can indeed be a little cryptic to the common man...

t_ch: 不过请放心，作为神人之间的纽带与桥梁，我能够为你将其解读为通俗易懂的语句。
t_en: But, please rest assured. As the bridge that connects the mortal and divine worlds, I will be able to interpret their words into something easier to comprehend.

t_ch: 真、真的吗？谢谢您，祭司大人！
t_en: Re—Really? Thank you, Sir Priest!

t_ch: 无需客气，这本就是我职责所在。不过…在此之前，请先向我展示你对命运泰坦的虔诚之心。
t_en: There is no need for gratitude. This is precisely my duty. However... before I can begin, I require you to demonstrate your spirit of piety to the Fate Titans.

t_ch: 虔诚之心…？我、我为了求取神谕，只身从奥德斯特里斯城邦来此，独自在大地兽背上辗转了二十个日夜，这样能够证明我的虔诚吗……
t_en: My spirit of piety...? I—I traveled to this place all the way from Odestris atop a dromas for twenty days and nights just to seek this prophecy. Is this enough to prove my piety?

t_ch: 你做得很好，孩子，但这些还不够。
t_en: You have done well, my child. But, this is not enough.

t_ch: 那…那我该怎么做呢，祭司大人？
t_en: Then... Then what am I to do, Lord Priest?

t_ch: 唉，这么说吧…您应当知道，进行祭祀时，只有当牺牲的鲜血泼洒在祭坛之上，神明才会向我们投下赞许的目光。
t_en: Ah, allow me to put it this way... You should understand that when one is carrying out a ritual, it is only when the sacrificial blood hits the altar that the gods will grace us with their approval.

t_ch: 故而作为一名希求神明垂青的虔诚信徒，你需得在塔兰顿的天秤中放上足够的分量。
t_en: As a devout disciple that desires to be favored by the gods, you will need to place enough weight atop the scales of Talanton.

t_ch: 命运三女神在上，孩子，你还在犹豫什么？难道你对父亲的孝心就只是口中的空谈？
t_en: Praise to the Three Titans of Fate, child, what are you waiting for? Is your filial piety to your father no more than lip service, after all?

t_ch: 不、我不是这个意思…只是…我是瞒着家里偷偷溜出来的，身上的钱财或许并不足以……
t_en: No, it's not that... It's just... I snuck out without the knowledge of my family. I fear the money I have on me may not be...

t_ch: 你误会了，孩子。神明从不曾被凡俗之财所取悦。你所需奉上的，仅是能够代表心意的重要之物。
t_en: You misunderstand me, child. The gods would never derive joy from the fortune of mortals. What you need to offer is something of importance to represent your intention.

t_ch: 重要之物…我、我有一条绿宝石项链，是母亲送给我的成年礼，价值…啊，抱歉，我不该用世俗的价值去亵渎神明……
t_en: Something of importance... I—I have an emerald necklace. It was a gift from my mother when I reached the age of majority. As for the price... Ah! Sorry, I should not blaspheme against the Gods with a mortal definition of value...

t_ch: 无妨，孩子，泰坦会宽恕你的无心之失。那么现在，请将项链交给我吧，让我来作出判断，它是否有资格陈列在女神们洁净的供台上。
t_en: Fear not, child, the Titans will overlook your innocent mistake. Now, hand over the necklace to me and I shall judge whether it is fit to be placed upon the pure altar of The Fates.

t_ch: …好、好的，我这就……
t_en: ...O—Okay, I'll just...

t_ch: 护卫呢，这里有人诈骗！
t_en: Guards! There's a scammer!

t_ch: 什——什么人？！竟敢在此口出狂言——哈，一身怪服的异乡客，却想叫卫队捉拿侍奉泰坦的祭司？当真可笑！
t_en: Wh—Who's there!? How dare you make such outrageous accusations in a place like — hah, an outsider clad in bizarre clothing wishes to summon the guards against a priest of the Titans? Laughable!

t_ch: 庆幸并无人听到你那愚蠢之词吧…若是卫队当真被唤来，此刻你恐怕已经因「诽谤圣女」的罪名被驱逐出雅努萨波利斯了！
t_en: Fortunately no one heard your foolish outburst... If the guards had actually been summoned, I fear you'd have already been cast out of Janusopolis for "defaming the Holy Maiden!"

t_ch: 你怎么又在忽悠人？
t_en: How are you still swindling people like this?

t_ch: 什——什么人？！竟敢在此口出狂言——啊，怎么是你？
t_en: Wh—Who's there!? How dare you make such outrageous accusations — ah, you again?

t_ch: 但即便是贵客，这等污蔑也着实太过冒犯了！
t_en: Even if you are an esteemed guest, this level of defamation is a complete violation!

t_ch: 竟有人敢在圣地之中公然侮辱神明的侍者——三相泰坦的名誉岂容如此玷污？纵使女神们向来公正仁慈，也不是尔等凡俗亵渎的藉口！
t_en: Someone would dare publicly besmirch the name of an attendant to the Goddesses in this holy place — how could one tarnish the reputation of Three Titans of Fate? Though the goddesses have always been merciful and kind, this is no excuse for your mortal blasphemy!

t_ch: 念你尚且是初犯，此次我便不再多计较。但今日看来不宜谈论神谕之事，就此告辞！
t_en: As this is your first offense, I will leave this matter here. However, it seems that our discussion of a prophecy will not be possible today. I bid you farewell!

t_ch: 啊？！请、请等等啊，祭司大人……
t_en: Huh!? P—Please wait, Lord Priest...

t_ch: 您…呃，您好，请问有什么事呢？
t_en: H—Hello, can I help you with something?

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 可以是可以…但我似乎并不认识您……
t_en: I guess so... But I don't believe I know you...

t_ch: 你在这里做什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 我…来此觐见圣女，希望能领受泰坦指示。
t_en: I... I came to see the Holy Maiden. I wish for guidance from the Titans.

t_ch: …呃…几个月前，家父曾来此求取过一则神谕。但自此以后，他开始变得郁郁寡欢起来，甚至时常整日闭门不出。
t_en: ...Agh... My father came to this place seeking a prophecy a few months ago. However, he has grown more and more depressed since that day. Now, he spends most of his days locked inside the house.

t_ch: 所以他问了什么？
t_en: What did he ask?

t_ch: 他…询问祭司们自己会在何时死去，得到的答案则是：「在艾格勒某次闭目之时」。
t_en: He... He asked the priests when he would die. The answer he received was: "When Aquila closes one of their eyes."

t_ch: 是啊，何其缥缈的说辞…可父亲却依旧因此变得焦躁多疑、惴惴不安。我们…姐姐们和母亲都很担心他。
t_en: Indeed, it's extremely vague... But my father is now overcome with anxiety and paranoia. We... My elder sister and my mother are all very worried about him.

t_ch: 所以即便从未远行过、即便怀着满心惶恐畏惧，我还是鼓起勇气，来到这命运泰坦垂迹的圣地，寻求一个解开父亲心结的可能性……
t_en: So, though I have never made a long journey like this before and my heart was filled with fear, I plucked up the courage to come to the holy land of the Fate Titans and find something that may ease my father's concerns...

t_ch: 你差点被那家伙骗了！
t_en: That guy almost scammed you!

t_ch: 可是…那可是女神们的祭司，是人神沟通的桥梁…如此神圣的大人真的会因为这种理由欺骗我吗……
t_en: But... He is a priest of the Goddesses, the bridge between the mortal and divine worlds... Do you really think that such a holy person would scam me just to steal a necklace...?

t_ch: 信我，他绝对在忽悠你！
t_en: Believe me, he was deceiving you!

t_ch: 可是…除了那位大人，其他的祭祀也都是这样的啊？
t_en: But... Does that mean other priests scam as he does?

t_ch: 他之前也这么忽悠过我！
t_en: He scammed me like this before as well!

t_ch: 可…如果是这样的话，那不恰巧说明了那位大人并没有骗人吗？
t_en: But... If that's true, does that not prove that they were not actually scamming anyone?

t_ch: 「在塔兰顿的天秤中放上足够的分量」…我记得之前家父在求取神谕的时候，也足足供奉了我们领地半年的收成……
t_en: "Place enough weight atop the scales of Talanton..." I remember when my father came in search of a prophecy, he offered up half a year's yield from our land...

t_ch: 啊，抱歉，我…我不是在反驳或是质疑您，也非常感谢您的善心！
t_en: Ah, sorry, I... I'm not trying to argue with you or interrogate you. I'm very grateful for your kindness!

t_ch: 只是…或许、或许其中有什么误会也说不定呢……
t_en: It's just... Maybe, maybe there has been some kind of misunderstanding...

t_ch: 那你之后有什么打算？
t_en: Well, what do you plan to do from here?

t_ch: 我…我打算继续请人为我解读神谕，毕竟我就是为此而来的……
t_en: I... I plan to keep searching for someone to interpret my prophecy. That was my whole reason for coming, after all...

t_ch: 你需要多留心。
t_en: You must be cautious.

t_ch: 小心神谕诈骗。
t_en: Beware of prophecy scammers.

t_ch: …哈哈，虽然这样的说法着实有些失礼，但我能感受到您的善意，所以…非常感谢。
t_en: ...Haha. That turn of phrase seems a little disrespectful but, I can sense your sincerity and kindness... Thank you so much.

t_ch: 刚刚到达雅努萨波利斯时，我其实也感到困惑不解…我曾以为神谕是泰坦赠与我们的、伟大又无私的关爱，而人们为此奉上牺牲则是出于对神明最纯洁的虔诚与敬重。
t_en: When I had just arrived in Janusopolis, I was actually quite confused and uncertain... I used to think that prophecies were a great and selfless love gifted to us by the Titans, and that the sacrifices people make were out of pure piety and respect.

t_ch: 但…现在我已经目睹过很多次其他人是如何求取神谕的了，他们都是如此…明码标价…甚至有时价高者得。
t_en: However... Now I have witnessed many times how it is people obtain their prophecies. The priests are all so... clear about needing payment... Some prophecies even end up as auctions.

t_ch: 这些都和我的想象完全不同，让人感到既矛盾又困惑…我曾就此询问过那些祭司，而他们回答我说：「在塔兰顿的威严面前，一切都需要代价。」
t_en: This is all completely different to what I imagined. I'm so conflicted and confused... I once asked some priests about this, and they replied: "Seeking an audience with the majesty of Talanton will always require a price."

t_ch: 我不知道这是否是所谓的*诈骗*…但即便确实是，对只能听取神谕、从来无力反抗的我们来说…虚假和真实又有什么区别呢……
t_en: I don't know if this is what you refer to as a "scam"... But, even if it is, to us, who have no power over our fate and are dependent on our prophecies... what difference is there between falsehood and reality...

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 啊，好的…也祝您在此得偿所愿！
t_en: Ah, alright... Then may you find what it is you are wishing for!

t_ch: 先不聊了，祝你好运。
t_en: I can't chat now. Good luck with everything.

t_ch: 啊，好的…也祝您在此得偿所愿！
t_en: Ah, alright... Then may you find what it is you are wishing for!

t_ch: 站住！此乃祭祀重地，你一身奇装异服，鬼鬼祟祟到处晃悠，有什么目的？圣女卫队呢，我卫队哪儿去了！
t_en: Stop right there! This is a sacred place! A place meant to deliver offerings unto the divine. What is your purpose here, sneaking around in those strange clothes? Where are the Holy Maiden's guards!?

t_ch: 堂堂祭司，怎么一身敏感肌。
t_en: I've never met such a sensitive priest before?

t_ch: 大胆，怎敢对我口出狂言！
t_en: How dare you make such outrageous accusations!

t_ch: 被我盘问，非但没有慌乱，竟还愈发狂妄，你到底是什么人？
t_en: Not only are you not startled in the face of my interrogation, but you even grow more arrogant!? Just who on earth are you?

t_ch: 你不是祭司么？神谕没告诉你？
t_en: Are you not a priest? Did the prophecy not tell you?

t_ch: 我…唯有雅努斯圣女才能传递神谕，我等祭司只需保护圣女、应她所需定期开启仪式即可。
t_en: I... Only the Holy Maiden is able to pass on prophecies. Priests like myself need only protect the Holy Maiden and carry out ceremonies when required.

t_ch: 再说了，这里每日都有无数外邦人进进出出，难道都要靠三相神谕验明来历？
t_en: What's more, we have countless outlanders coming and going through these halls every day. You think we should rely on the tripartite prophecy to identify every last one?

t_ch: 我来雅城当县长。
t_en: I have come to Janusopolis to serve as a magistrate.

t_ch: 县长？你可真是装糊涂的天才！少说胡话，圣女卫队的锋刃可不和你开玩笑。
t_en: A magistrate!? You are truly a master of smoke and mirrors! Enough nonsense, the blades of the Holy Maiden's guards will not join you in your jokes.

t_ch: 我乃银河球棒侠！
t_en: I am the Galactic Baseballer that roams the endless sea of stars!

t_ch: 翁法罗斯可没有哪条河叫做银河，冥河倒是有。怎么，你在冥河里游过？
t_en: Everyone in Amphoreus knows that one cannot traverse the sea of stars! The River of Souls is always available, though. Have you tried a dip?

t_ch: 我是塔拉萨波利斯的贵族。
t_en: I am an aristocrat of Thalassopolis.

t_ch: 塔拉萨波利斯？从未听说过的城邦。你也是来求取神谕的吗？
t_en: Thalassopolis? I've never heard of this city. Are you here for a prophecy, too?

t_ch: 嗯…这时代确实没有流民和骗子能够穿得这样华丽精致，也许是我误会你了。
t_en: Hmm... It is true that a refugee or scammer would not be draped in such extravagant finery. Perhaps there has been a misunderstanding on my part.

t_ch: 不过，既是异邦贵族，可有能验明身份的证物？例如…贵邦的珍稀特产？
t_en: However, if you are an outlander aristocrat, do you have something that serves to prove your identity? For instance... some rare souvenir from your foreign land?

t_ch: 你似乎能听懂，但是你不想听懂，你眯起眼睛看着这位道貌岸然的祭司。祭司笑了笑，向你伸出手来。
t_en: Though you understand what he is getting at, you decide that you'd rather play dumb. You squint your eyes at the sanctimonious priest. The priest smiles and reaches out a hand.

t_ch: 说得更明白些…阁下，若你有心求取神谕，也总该向三相神殿表露些诚意吧？
t_en: Let me make it clearer for you... My Lord, if you wish to obtain your prophecy, you must show your piety to the Temple of the Three Fates. Don't you agree?

t_ch: 那我就让你开开眼。
t_en: Then let me show you something special.

t_ch: 但是，我拒绝。
t_en: Thanks, I hate it.

t_ch: 那么，阁下，我爱莫能助：天底下并无免费的神谕。愿{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}揭示你的路径。
t_en: Well then, My Lord, I cannot help you. There is no such thing as a free prophecy in this world. May the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} guide your path.

t_ch: 这、这是何物？来命运三相殿奉上贡品、求取神谕的人潮比{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的海浪还要汹涌，可是我至今未曾见到与这相似的东西……
t_en: Wh—What is this object? The waves of people that come and make offerings to the Temple of the Three Fates dwarf even the waves of {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} in their volume. Yet, I have never seen an item like this before...

t_ch: 还请阁下接受我诚挚的歉意。命运三相殿地处神圣，圣女又手无缚鸡之力，我等祭司不得不严防死守。
t_en: Please accept my sincerest apologies, My Lord. The Temple of the Three Fates is a sacred location, and the Holy Maiden is powerless to protect it. We must keep it under tight guard.

t_ch: 命运泰坦们已经知晓您的诚意，我们会为你求取一份神谕。愿您的家乡——塔拉萨波利斯——平安无虞。
t_en: The Fate Titans have recognized your piety. We will obtain a prophecy for you. May your home — Thalassopolis — long remain safe and sound.

t_ch: 祭司笑眯眯地将你递出的东西收入囊中。那么，命运泰坦要如何占卜一处虚构之地的未来呢？你对此颇为好奇。
t_en: The priest stuffs the item you handed over into his pocket with a gleeful grin on his face. Well then, how will the Fate Titans divine a future for a place that you made up? Your curiosity is truly piqued.

t_ch: 也许等些时间再来找他，就能得到有趣的答案了。
t_en: Perhaps if you give him some time, he will have an interesting answer waiting for you.

t_ch: 那么，阁下，我爱莫能助：天底下并无免费的神谕。愿{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}揭示你的路径。
t_en: Well then, My Lord, I cannot help you. There is no such thing as a free prophecy in this world. May the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} guide your path.

t_ch: 阁下，莫非您回心转意，想向三相神殿表露诚心、求取神谕？
t_en: My Lord, could it be that you have had a change of heart and wish to show your piety to the Temple of the Three Fates in exchange for a prophecy?

t_ch: 我想听神谕。
t_en: I want to hear my prophecy.

t_ch: 别想捞一个子儿。
t_en: You're not getting a penny from me.

t_ch: 那么，阁下，我还是那句话：天底下并无免费的神谕。愿{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}揭示你的路径。
t_en: Very well, My Lord. Then I must remind you: There is no such thing as a free prophecy in this world. May the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} guide your path.

t_ch: 这，这是何物？来命运三相殿奉上贡品、求取神谕的人潮比法吉娜的海浪还要汹涌，可是我至今未曾见到与这相似的东西……
t_en: Wh—What is this object? The waves of people that come and make offerings to the Temple of the Three Fates dwarf even the waves of Phagousa in their volume. Yet, I have never seen an item like this before...

t_ch: 还请阁下接受我诚挚的歉意。命运三相殿地处神圣，圣女又手无缚鸡之力，我等祭司不得不严防死守。
t_en: Please accept my sincerest apologies, My Lord. The Temple of the Three Fates is a sacred location, and the Holy Maiden is powerless to protect it. We must keep it under tight guard.

t_ch: 命运泰坦们已经知晓您的诚意，我们会为你求取一份神谕。愿您的家乡——塔拉萨波利斯——平安无虞。
t_en: The Fate Titans have recognized your piety. We will obtain a prophecy for you. May your home — Thalassopolis — long remain safe and sound.

t_ch: 祭司笑眯眯地将你递出的东西收入囊中。那么，命运泰坦要如何占卜一处虚构之地的未来呢？你对此颇为好奇。
t_en: The priest stuffs the item you handed over into his pocket with a gleeful grin on his face. Well then, how will the Fate Titans divine a future for a place that you made up? Your curiosity is truly piqued.

t_ch: 也许等些时间再来找他，就能得到有趣的答案了。
t_en: Perhaps if you give him some time, he will have an interesting answer waiting for you.

t_ch: 那么，阁下，我还是那句话：天底下并无免费的神谕。愿{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}揭示你的路径。
t_en: Very well, My Lord. Then I must remind you: There is no such thing as a free prophecy in this world. May the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} guide your path.

t_ch: 阁下，有幸再度相见。上回你给我的珍宝都已纳与命运泰坦们了，不知阁下找我又有何事？
t_en: My Lord, it is an honor to see you again. The treasure that you bestowed to me before has been accepted by the Fate Titans. Is there something that I can help you with?

t_ch: 雅努斯是个怎样的泰坦？
t_en: What kind of Titan is Janus?

t_ch: 万径之门，雅努斯，门径之泰坦，命运三泰坦中的长姊。它揭示城邦未来、指引万民前路，于众泰坦中堪称最为完美的一尊。
t_en: The Gate of Infinity, Janus, is the Passage Titan and eldest sister of the Three Titans of Fate. They show the future of each city and guide the paths of the people. They are even referred to as the most perfect among all Titans.

t_ch: 与它的亘古辉煌相比，其它的泰坦简直…礼貌地说，黯淡无光。
t_en: When compared to their eternal glory, the other Titans are... to put it politely, a little lackluster.

t_ch: 不如刻法勒全知全能。
t_en: They are not all-knowing and all-powerful like Kephale.

t_ch: 神话里对刻法勒的赞誉，不过是由成功者书写的历史罢了。所谓全知全能，皆因奥赫玛是当今翁法罗斯最鼎盛的城邦。
t_en: They say that history is written by the winners, and that is precisely what has happened with the constant praise of Kephale in mythology. This so-called all-knowing and all-powerful nature comes from nothing more than the fact that Okhema is the most prospering city in Amphoreus.

t_ch: 倘有一日我雅努萨波利斯成为唯一正统的泰坦之领，必会还正史一片清朗。
t_en: If Janusopolis were to one day become the only legitimate Titan territory, I'm sure that the historical accounts would be suitably cleared.

t_ch: 我都想…整理好了，翁法罗斯正史第一章，万径之门开天辟地……
t_en: I want to... I've already prepared what I would write. Historical account of Amphoreus, chapter one: The Gate of Infinity splits the heavens and the earth...

t_ch: 当心尼卡多利让你飞起来。
t_en: Nikador will have your head for saying that.

t_ch: 尼卡多利，色厉胆薄、好勇无谋，在泰坦中和扎格列斯坐一桌，不值一提。
t_en: Nikador looks ferocious, but has no spine. They're all brawn and no brain. They sit at the same table as Zagreus and are barely worth a mention.

t_ch: 等悬锋人打过来你就老实了。
t_en: You'll change your tune when the Kremnoans get here.

t_ch: 我是悬锋间谍，这就回报出兵。
t_en: I'm a Kremnoan spy. I will report back to send troops here.

t_ch: 什么？…吓唬谁呢，阁下，悬锋人野蛮无忌，怎可能穿得惯你身上这样严严实实的衣服。
t_en: What? ...Who are you trying to scare? My Lord, the Kremnoans are savages. There is no way they would be adorned with such elaborate finery as you are.

t_ch: 雅努萨波利斯筑有命运三相殿，即使狂妄如悬锋人，也不敢轻易冒犯命运泰坦之所在。
t_en: The Temple of the Three Fates is located in Janusopolis. Not even the crazed Kremnoans would dare offend the home of the Fate Titans without proper consideration.

t_ch: 退一万步说，即使悬锋人真的对雅努萨波利斯图谋不轨，只要我们以神谕统合众邦，悬锋城又有何惧？
t_en: Even if the Kremnoans did have a plot against Janusopolis, we need simply unite the nations through our prophecies. What is there to fear about Castrum Kremnos?

t_ch: 我看瑟希斯和墨涅塔挺亮的。
t_en: I think Cerces and Mnestia shine pretty bright.

t_ch: 呵！坊间遍是这两尊泰坦厮混的野史，一个是木头脑袋，另一个因爱失智，实在上不了台面。
t_en: Hah! Everyone has heard the stories of these two Titans and their frolicking. One of their brains is as dense as wood, and the other is blinded by love. They have no place on the stage of Titans.

t_ch: 讲些有关圣女的事吧。
t_en: Tell me about the Holy Maiden.

t_ch: 阁下是从那些平民口中得知了有关红发圣女的事迹么？
t_en: I assume you have learned of the red-haired Holy Maiden from the common folk of Janusopolis?

t_ch: 要我说…堂堂命运泰坦的传谕圣女，却总想着去与平民接触，这种事成何体统！
t_en: If you ask me... I find it simply outrageous that she is the great Holy Maiden, fated to bear the prophecies of the Fate Titans, and all she wants to do is talk with common folk!

t_ch: 前代圣女尤为我行我素，还干过把贡品送给平民吃的事情，真是渎职。
t_en: The previous Holy Maiden was particularly insistent on doing things her way. She even gave Titan food offerings to the citizens! It was a complete misuse of power.

t_ch: 好在我们对现任圣女管束较为严苛，大部分平民都只知有圣女，未见过圣女样貌。
t_en: Fortunately, we have imposed harsher restrictions on the current Holy Maiden. The majority of citizens only know of the Holy Maiden's existence. They have never actually seen what she looks like.

t_ch: 这珍宝像进了你私人的腰包。
t_en: I saw you stuff my treasure into your pocket.

t_ch: …我就不瞒着阁下了，命运三泰坦对贡品并非照单全收，妥善处理多余的贡品也是祭司的职责。
t_en: ...I shall not deceive you, My Lord. The Three Titans of Fate do not personally accept all offerings. It is the duty of a priest to make sure that all excess offerings are handled properly.

t_ch: 有时候我们甚至要自掏腰包，补上合适的贡品、为友邦求取神谕。
t_en: Sometimes we even have to pay out of our own pockets to make up a suitable offering and obtain prophecies for our allies.

t_ch: 我一心只为雅努萨波利斯，客观上也确实需要替自己赢取些钱财造势，让我能在教会中为命运泰坦们做更多的事。
t_en: I am truly dedicated to Janusopolis alone. It is an objective fact that I also need to earn a certain amount of fortune and renown for myself that I am able to do more for the Fate Titans within the church.

t_ch: 须知，并非每位祭司都像我这般痛快真诚，城府深者大有人在。要是让那些家伙垄断了求取神谕的权力…对雅努萨波利斯断不是好事。
t_en: You must know that not all priests are as forthright and sincere as I am. There are many that are far more shrewd and subtle. If these priests were able to monopolize the power to obtain prophecies from the Titans... That would spell trouble for Janusopolis.

t_ch: 神谕将如何指引我的故乡？
t_en: How will the prophecy guide my homeland?

t_ch: 神谕的原文晦涩难懂，我便替您翻译作较为通俗易懂的语句吧——
t_en: The prophecies are often vague and hard to understand. I will translate it and make it easier for you to understand —

t_ch: 欧洛尼斯揭开遥远之处的帷幕，塔兰顿以天秤宣判尔等无罪，雅努斯指引如雾命运之迷津……
t_en: Oronyx lifts the distant veil, the scale of Talanton proclaims your innocence, and Janus guides those who are lost in the misty maze of destiny...

t_ch: 塔拉萨波利斯唷，命运只如无足之鸟，唯有三相所在之处方为归巢。
t_en: Oh, Thalassopolis! Fate is like a bird with no legs. It can only nest in the land of the Three Fates.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯已攥上你的脚踝…务要循着幻影渡河，寻回城邦的未来……
t_en: The cold grasp of Thanatos has grasped your ankle... You must follow the phantom across the river if you are to find a future for your city.

t_ch: 你这神谕太假了。
t_en: The prophecy is nonsense.

t_ch: 太好了，是神谕，我们有救了。
t_en: Great, we have a prophecy! We're saved!

t_ch: 三相神殿的神谕绝对真实，也从未出过错。阁下还是尽早返乡，将贵邦从黯淡的命运中拯救出来吧。
t_en: The prophecy of the Temple of the Three Fates is absolute, and they have never been mistaken before. My Lord, you must return home immediately and rescue your home from its dim fate.

t_ch: 你看着祭司煞有介事的样子，努力把笑忍住了。
t_en: You try your best to hold back your laughter as you watch the priest putting on their dramatic rendition.

t_ch: 塔拉萨波利斯的贵客，切勿心急，我们还尚未向命运泰坦们求取神谕。
t_en: Esteemed guest from Thalassopolis, please be patient. We are still yet to consult the Fate Titans for your prophecy.

t_ch: 不如稍等些日子再来找我，命运泰坦们一定会给您最正确的指引。
t_en: Perhaps you should come and find me in a few days. I know that the Titans will provide you with the most accurate guidance possible.

t_ch: 就聊这些吧。
t_en: Let's leave it there for now.

t_ch: 有缘再见，塔拉萨波利斯的贵客。愿{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}揭示你的路径。
t_en: May we meet again, esteemed guest from Thalassopolis. May the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} guide your path.

t_ch: 啊呀，今天也是不错的一天呢…你是来参观的客人吧？真不巧，你来得迟了些。
t_en: Ah, what a splendid day indeed... Are you here to visit? You're a tad late, unfortunately.

t_ch: 小甜饼已经派完了，你要是早半刻来，正好能赶上最后一盘呢。
t_en: The last of the sweet treats has just been given away. Had you arrived a moment sooner, you could have snagged the final batch.

t_ch: 你是神殿的厨娘？
t_en: Are you the temple's chef?

t_ch: 不，我只是名普通的侍女，只不过有幸服侍过圣女大人。在神殿里什么活我都干过，扫地、洗衣、侍奉祭祀……
t_en: No, I'm merely a humble maid, though I've had the privilege of serving the Holy Maiden. I've undertaken various tasks here in the temple — sweeping, laundry, assisting in sacred rituals...

t_ch: 不过我最爱做的，还是烤点心。人在难过的时候，来块小甜饼，心情都会好很多。所以，神殿里那么多庄严的事，我偏生对这种小事最上心。
t_en: But what I love most is baking. There's something about a sweet that can brighten a gloomy day. Amid all the grave responsibilities in the temple, it's these little joys that I hold dear.

t_ch: 这是什么特别的甜点吗？
t_en: Is there something special about this dessert?

t_ch: 是我的独门秘方，多年来一直深受神殿上下的青睐。每次我打开烤炉，这香味就把勾来一大片馋虫。
t_en: It's my secret recipe, cherished by all in the temple for years. Every time I open the oven, the aroma alone draws a crowd, all eager for a taste.

t_ch: 说来也是好笑，我在神殿里侍奉了这么久，大家记住我的不是我这个人，反而是我烤出来的小甜饼。
t_en: The funny thing is, after all these years serving in the temple, people remember not so much me, but more so the sweet treats I bake.

t_ch: 打扰了，我只是路过。
t_en: Sorry to intrude. I was just passing by.

t_ch: 哎呀，年纪大了就爱唠叨，但愿没耽误你的行程，年轻人。
t_en: Oh dear, I do tend to ramble on as I get older. I hope I haven't kept you from your plans, youngster.

t_ch: 我至今还记得，当年的圣女大人边吃边称赞的样子，她说，这甜点吃起来很暖心。
t_en: I still recall how the Holy Maiden once savored these desserts, calling them "heartwarming."

t_ch: 不知不觉这么多年过去了，可我感觉这件事仿佛就在昨天发生似的，哎……
t_en: Years have flown by, but it feels like it all happened just yesterday. Time does fly...

t_ch: 你在这工作了很久吗？
t_en: Have you been working here long?

t_ch: 是啊，自我刚懂事起，就被派过来服侍圣女大人了。往后，我待在神殿工作了几十年。
t_en: Indeed. I was sent here to aid the Holy Maiden since I was old enough to walk. I've spent decades within these walls.

t_ch: 那时候我的头发还又黑又亮，脸上也没有皱纹，哪像现在。
t_en: I had sleek, jet-black hair and not a wrinkle in sight back then. Quite a different story from today.

t_ch: …给我听饿了。
t_en: ...I am hungry now.

t_ch: 哈，可能是这里还有些残余的香味吧。毕竟，我都用的是最好的食材。
t_en: Ha, perhaps it's the remnant scents of today's baking. I only use the finest ingredients, after all.

t_ch: 每次早早起来，筛出最好的面粉，过滤最纯净的蜂蜜，那股香甜味我自己闻到都忍不住口水直流。
t_en: Every morning, I wake early to sift the finest flour and strain the purest honey. The sweet aroma is so tempting, even I can't help but start to drool.

t_ch: 这些年来，我见过太多人走进神殿，有些人是为了求得泰坦们的神谕，有些人是为了寻找些什么。
t_en: Over the years, I've seen many come to seek the Titans' prophecy or search for something profound within these temple walls.

t_ch: 但有些人只是想散散心，或者找个说话的地方…每个人都有自己的故事，
t_en: But some might just need a place to think or a listening ear... Everyone has their own tale to tell.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，虽然我不知道你是为了什么，但要是有时间，可以常来跟老婆子说说话，喝杯热茶，吃点小甜饼，或许能从中找到你想要的答案。
t_en: {F#My girl}{M#My boy}, though your quest is unknown to me, feel free to stop by anytime for a chat over hot tea and treats. You might just find what you're looking for.

t_ch: 啊呀，又见到你了，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，我就说，今天也是不错的一天呢。
t_en: Ah, there you are again, {F#my girl}{M#my boy}. I knew today was going to be good.

t_ch: 是有什么事吗，还是说想吃点小甜饼？我刚把面团揉好放进烤炉，要不等一会儿？
t_en: Do you need something, or are you craving some sweets? I just put a fresh batch in the oven. Care to wait a bit?

t_ch: 我想找你聊聊天。
t_en: I was hoping to chat with you.

t_ch: 好，聊什么都可以，没想到还有人愿意听老婆子我唠叨。
t_en: Sure, we can chat about anything. It's not often someone's interested in an old lady's ramblings.

t_ch: 能教我做甜饼吗？
t_en: Can you show me how to make these sweet treats?

t_ch: 哎呀，终于有人问这个了，大家都只喜欢吃，一个个的都懒得做。其实很简单的，就是要用心些，来，我教你，可记好咯。
t_en: Ah, finally someone wants to learn this! Everyone just loves eating, but no one wants to bother making it. It's quite simple — you just need to put in some effort. Come on. I'll teach you, so pay attention.

t_ch: 首先面粉要过筛，蜂蜜要选最纯净的。然后嘛，做点心得用心，揉面团的时候要根据不同人的口味来，烤制的火候也是。
t_en: First, sift the flour well and choose only the purest honey. Then, you need to put your heart into the process of baking. It's crucial to tailor the dough to suit individual tastes and to get the baking just right.

t_ch: 最重要的是，烤制的时候记得向泰坦们祈祷，为接下来要品尝小甜饼的人送上你诚挚的祝福，这个步骤千万不能少，要不然吃起来就不是那么个味道了。
t_en: Most importantly, when baking, always offer a prayer to the Titans, blessing those who will enjoy your treats. Skipping this step means they just won't taste as delightful.

t_ch: 听说你服侍过圣女？
t_en: Did you serve the Holy Maiden once?

t_ch: 这个嘛…我曾被指派为以前某位圣女的侍女，哎，说是圣女，其实不过是个小姑娘。吃点心的时候甚至会偷偷藏一块，我问她为什么，她说想留着跟我分享。
t_en: Yes, I once served as a maid for a previous Holy Maiden. Despite her title, she was just a young girl. She'd even sneak a piece of dessert away while having snacks, and when I asked her why, she said she wanted to save it to share with me later.

t_ch: 哎，每次看到她那个眼神，我的心都感觉要化了。虽然那时候我还没成家，但是…咳咳，说句大逆不道的话——从那时候起我就觉得她跟我的孩子没什么两样。
t_en: Her gaze always made my heart melt. Even though I had no children of my own at that time... *cough* It may sound sacrilegious, but I felt as if she were no different from a daughter to me.

t_ch: 当然，这些话咱们私下说说就算了，你可千万别传出去，影响不好。
t_en: But let's keep this conversation between us. It wouldn't be right to spread such personal reflections.

t_ch: 你平常都在神殿里做什么？
t_en: What do you normally do around the temple?

t_ch: 能做什么呀，我能做的无非是些家长里短的事咯。
t_en: What else can I do? My days are filled with simple, essential chores.

t_ch: 打扫神殿，然后准备点心。有时候年轻的祭司们会来找我们老家伙聊天，问问以前的事。闲下来就在这儿坐着，给路过的人倒杯茶。
t_en: Cleaning the temple, then prepare treats. Sometimes the young priests seek wisdom from us elders. When it's quiet, I just sit here, offering tea to those who pass by.

t_ch: 日子虽然平淡，但我觉得很充实，神殿就是我的家。
t_en: Life might be simple, but it's fulfilling. This temple is my home.

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: I don't have much else to say.

t_ch: 没事没事，你先忙你的吧，这年头能有人愿意听老婆子唠叨已经很难得了。
t_en: No worries. You carry on with your day. It's rare these days for anyone to care for an old lady's stories.

t_ch: 没事，我只是来看看你。
t_en: It's okay. I just came to check on you.

t_ch: 哎，你这孩子，可太客气了。要是觉得累了，可以随时过来坐坐。
t_en: Ah, you're too kind. If you ever need a break, feel free to come over and talk to me anytime.

t_ch: 愿泰坦们保佑你，年轻人…也保佑那些迷茫的人们。
t_en: May the Titans bless you, youngster... and all those who wander in search of clarity.

t_ch: …所以我们今天也要好好努力才行呀～啊，有人来了。
t_en: ...So, let's make the most of today — oh, it looks like someone's approaching.

t_ch: 你好，看你的样子，是外地来的朋友吗？我叫达娜叶，这一带我挺熟的，需要我带你到处参观一下吗？
t_en: Hello, you seem to be from out of town. My name's Danae, and I know this area well. Do you need a guide?

t_ch: 你还挺热心的。
t_en: You're quite hospitable.

t_ch: 啊…没有啦，都是些力所能及的小事，不算什么。
t_en: Ah, it's nothing. These are just small acts within my power. Nothing noteworthy.

t_ch: 呃，收费吗？
t_en: Uh, does it cost anything?

t_ch: 啊？没有没有，我不是神殿的工作人员…当然是免费啦！
t_en: Ah? No, I don't work for the temple... It's certainly free!

t_ch: 打扰了，我想自己逛逛。
t_en: Sorry, but I'd like to explore on my own.

t_ch: 噢噢，没问题，那就不打扰你啦。
t_en: Oh, sure thing. I won't bother you any further.

t_ch: 不必拘谨啦，母亲从小教我的——要像圣女大人那样，有余力就去帮助别人。
t_en: No need for formality. My mom taught me from a young age to be like the Holy Maiden — always lend a hand whenever possible.

t_ch: 所以在闲暇时候，我们一家还会过来做些义务劳作，像是清理杂草啊、擦灰什么的。
t_en: So, my family volunteer here in our spare time, tackling chores like pulling weeds and dusting.

t_ch: 我从小就在神殿附近长大，附近的人都认识我，你要是不放心，可以向他们打听打听。
t_en: I've lived near the temple my whole life, and everyone around here knows me. If you have any doubts, you're welcome to ask the locals about me.

t_ch: 你的家教真好。
t_en: You were raised well.

t_ch: 这个…怎么说呢，虽然爸爸听到会很高兴，但我们是为了报恩——很久以前，母亲还是个小女孩的时候，欠过圣女大人的人情。
t_en: Well, my dad would be thrilled, but we're here to honor an old debt — when my mother was just a little girl, she owed a significant favor to the Holy Maiden.

t_ch: 她小时候生活非常困苦，经常饿肚子。有一天实在饿得受不了，就偷偷溜进神殿想找点吃的，结果就被抓现行了。
t_en: Growing up, my mother faced severe hardships and often went without food. One particularly desperate day, she snuck into the temple hoping to find something to eat but was caught red-handed.

t_ch: 那时候，偷窃神殿供品可是重罪。但当时的圣女大人不仅没有惩罚她，问清事由后还给了她满满一大包的小甜饼，让她平安回家了。
t_en: Back in those days, stealing from the temple was considered a grave offense. However, the Holy Maiden at the time chose not to punish her. After listening to her plight, she even gave my mother a bag full of sweet treats, allowing her to return home safely.

t_ch: 自那以后，妈妈就把家搬到了神殿附近。她总说，既然受过圣女大人的帮助，就应当把她的恩情传下去。
t_en: My mother decided to move near the temple after that. She always maintained that since she had benefitted from the Holy Maiden's generosity, it was her responsibility to continue spreading that goodness.

t_ch: 能为我介绍下这里吗？
t_en: Can you show me around?

t_ch: 当然可以呀，我自幼在这附近长大，这里的屋檐、石柱，甚至墙角的每一寸青苔我都再熟悉不过了。
t_en: Absolutely! I've grown up here and know every part of this place — the eaves, the columns, and even the moss on the walls.

t_ch: 你看门口的台阶，表面有些断裂的痕迹，那是因为朝圣的信徒太多，以至于石阶不堪重负——
t_en: See the steps at the entrance? They're a bit worn and cracked, a testament to the many pilgrims that have passed through here —

t_ch: 据说在很久以前，命运三泰坦会在此现身，回应人们的祈祷。不过那都是很久远的事了，现在基本都由祭司们和圣女大人负责传达它们的神谕。
t_en: Legend has it that, centuries ago, the Three Titans of Fate would manifest here to respond to the prayers of the people. But those days are long gone. It's primarily the priests and the Holy Maiden who relay their prophecies now.

t_ch: 对了，我看你好像是远道而来，走了很久吧？要不要先去休息一下，需要向导的话可以随时来找我~
t_en: You've traveled a long way. Why not rest up for now? If you need a guide, I'm usually around.

t_ch: 是你呀，我记得…你不是本地人对吧？找我有什么事，是需要向导吗？
t_en: I remember now — you're not from around here, are you? What brings you to me? Do you need a guide?

t_ch: 我想找你聊聊…
t_en: I was hoping we could talk...

t_ch: 好呀，你想聊些什么？
t_en: Sure. What would you like to discuss?

t_ch: 能讲讲你母亲的事吗？
t_en: Could you share more about your mother?

t_ch: 妈妈的事啊…那个关于圣女大人是如何拯救她的故事，我听过无数遍了，但是每次听她讲，我都觉得特别温暖。
t_en: The story of how the Holy Maiden saved my mother... I've heard it countless times, but it still warms my heart every time I hear it.

t_ch: 她那时候还小，在那个冰冷的雪天，家里连一粒面包屑都没有，她在神殿外面徘徊了很久，在墙根下发现一个很小的洞，最后实在饿得受不了了，就钻了进去……
t_en: She was very young. It was a bitterly cold, snowy day, and she didn't even have a single crumb to eat at home. She lingered outside the temple for a long time before discovering a tiny hole at the base of the wall. Overwhelmed by hunger, she crawled through...

t_ch: 她想爬到神像脚下偷点供品，但是被看管的祭司发现了，妈妈说她吓得浑身发抖，以为自己肯定死定了。结果祭司将她带到审讯室的时候，圣女正巧也在。
t_en: She intended to steal some offerings at the statue's feet but was caught by a priest. My mom told me she trembled with fear, convinced her fate was sealed. However, when the priest took her to the interrogation room, by chance, the Holy Maiden was also present.

t_ch: 圣女轻声问：「…你多久没吃饭了？怎么这么瘦？」然后就请她吃了自己的茶点，确认她恢复力气后，塞了一大包点心让她背回家。
t_en: The Holy Maiden spoke gently. "How long have you been without food? You are thin as a stick?" After offering my mother some of her snacks and ensuring my mother had enough strength, she packed a large bag of treats for my mother to take home.

t_ch: 圣女大人还叮嘱，要她好好照顾自己。这么多年来，母亲一直想报答，可惜普通人没法轻易与圣女接触。母亲只好把这份感激之情向我们传承下去。
t_en: The Holy Maiden also urged her to take care of herself. Over the years, my mother has always wanted to repay the kindness, but meeting the Holy Maiden isn't something we can simply do. Instead, she instilled that deep gratitude in us.

t_ch: 你每天在这里忙些什么？
t_en: What do you usually do here each day?

t_ch: 没事的时候，我会帮忙打扫下附近，比如顺手拔点杂草，刮刮外墙上的青苔什么的。
t_en: When I have some spare time, I pitch in with tidying up around here — pulling weeds, scraping moss off the walls, and the like.

t_ch: 除此以外，你应该知道的——就是给游客指指路，担任下义务向导什么的。
t_en: Besides that — as you probably know — I help visitors find their way around, acting as a volunteer guide.

t_ch: 毕竟这里的路挺复杂，神殿里还有很多地方是禁止入内的，刚来的时候容易走丢，万一惹上什么麻烦就不好了。
t_en: Given the complexity of the paths here, and with many areas being off-limits, it's easy for newcomers to get lost, which could lead to trouble.

t_ch: 你有遇到什么有趣的人吗？
t_en: Have you met any interesting people here?

t_ch: 唔，说不上有趣，毕竟来这里的人形形色色，各有各的故事。
t_en: Well, not necessarily interesting, as everyone who comes here has their own story to tell.

t_ch: 有专程来朝圣的信徒，有研究历史的学者，也有来探索未来运势的商人。
t_en: The temple attracts devout pilgrims, scholars studying history, and merchants looking to glimpse their future fortunes.

t_ch: 要我说，这里面最有趣的或许还是侍奉泰坦们的那些祭司吧…还有圣女大人，不知道在他们眼里，我们这样的普通信徒是什么样。
t_en: I find the priests who serve the Titans and the Holy Maiden the most intriguing of all... It makes me curious how they view us mere regular followers.

t_ch: 先聊到这儿吧，告辞。
t_en: Let's stop here for now. Goodbye.

t_ch: 好，路上小心哦。要是在这儿遇到了麻烦，需要帮助什么的，记得来找我～
t_en: Alright, take care. If you run into trouble and need help, remember to look me up.

t_ch: 没事，我只是路过。
t_en: It's nothing. I was just passing through.

t_ch: 噢，有机会再聊。要是在这儿遇到了麻烦，需要帮助什么的，记得来找我哦～
t_en: Oh, let's catch up in the future. If you run into trouble and need help, remember to look me up.

t_ch: 想聊几句吗？
t_en: Feel like talking for a bit?

t_ch: 啊…没问题。要聊聊奥赫玛推理协会的故事吗？我的那几位朋友…他们都是很好的人。
t_en: Ah, sure. Would you like to hear about the Okhema Deduction Society? My friends... they are wonderful people.

t_ch: 聊聊尼科马库斯。
t_en: Tell me about Nicomachus.

t_ch: 尼科马库斯比我们的年龄都大一些，性格也更沉稳。他是「绳结学派」贤人的首席秘书，负责招募新人的工作。他标准很严格，听说上任以来总共也只招了两人。
t_en: Nicomachus is a bit older than us and is known for his calm and collected nature. He holds the position of head secretary for the Nodist Sage, in charge of recruitment. Known for his stringent standards, he's reputed to have recruited only two people since his tenure began.

t_ch: 尼科马库斯加入推理协会的原因，说起来有点好笑——他是为了和科洛埃小姐有更多私下相处的时间才报名的。不过他确实博学多才，看过的故事比我们都多。
t_en: He joined the Deduction Society partly to spend more personal time with Miss Clio, which is... quite a ridiculous reason. He is a capable scholar at heart, though, and he's read more than any of us.

t_ch: 说到他的趣事…啊，这还是安德烈告诉我的。有次尼科马库斯和一个「智种学派」的学者公开辩论，谈到泰坦的权能是否至高无上时，他竟然这样提供论据——
t_en: As for an amusing tale about him — this one comes from Andre. Once, Nicomachus was in a public debate with a Nousporist scholar about whether the Titans' powers are absolute. Nicomachus offered this kind of argument —

t_ch: 「自然对数的圆周率倍的单位虚数幂再加一等于零，所以泰坦的权能至高无上，证毕！」
t_en: "The power of pi times the natural logarithm, raised to the power of the imaginary unit plus one, equals zero, thereby proving the absolute power of the Titan. Q.E.D."

t_ch: ——后来大家发现他那时是急着想上厕所。
t_en: Turns out, he just really needed a bathroom break.

t_ch: 聊聊安德烈亚斯。
t_en: Tell me about Andreas.

t_ch: 安德烈——啊，也就是安德烈亚斯，他是我们几个之中最年轻的，和我一样都是奥赫玛土生土长的居民。他是刻法勒的信徒，因为家里有个不敬神的哥哥而经常头痛不已。
t_en: Andreas is the youngest of us, born and raised in Okhema just like me. He's a devoted disciple of Kephale, though his brother's lack of reverence frequently gives him headaches.

t_ch: 安德烈是从传信石版上看到我发的会员招募信息以后加入的，他虽然很虔诚，但生活并不古板，喜欢参加这些类似我们这样奇奇怪怪的活动小组。
t_en: He joined us after seeing my recruitment post on the teleslate. Though deeply faithful, he isn't old-fashioned and enjoys participating in unusual activities like ours.

t_ch: 他还是奥赫玛棍球协会的成员，据说打棍球的水平比「曳石学派」的很多人还要好…不过这个也有可能是他自吹自擂的结果。
t_en: He's also a member of the Okhema Stickball Society, rumored to play better than many from the Helkolithists, though that might be his own brag.

t_ch: 不论如何，我本希望在他加入之后，协会能够吸引到一些更年轻的同好，从而可以开展更多的活动…唉。
t_en: I had hoped his joining would bring in more young people so we could do more together, but alas.

t_ch: 聊聊科洛埃。
t_en: Tell me about Clio.

t_ch: 科洛埃小姐算是协会的创始成员。事情的起因是她有一次来浴场玩，正好碰见我和几个人在图书室读推理小说。她和我们刚好喜欢同一个作家，就这么聊起来了……
t_en: Miss Clio is practically a founding member of the society. It all started when she came to the baths and bumped into a few of us reading detective novels in the library. She happened to like the same author as us, and that kicked off our conversation...

t_ch: 后来我们几个在浴场偶遇的同好就互相留了联系方式，成立了这个自封为「奥赫玛推理协会」的活动社。
t_en: Then, since we kept seeing each other at the baths, our group exchanged contacts and established what we now proudly call the "Okhema Deduction Society."

t_ch: 因为没有什么定期活动，随着时间推移，不时有旧人退出、偶尔才有新人加入…到今年就只剩下了我们四个活跃分子。
t_en: Without regular gatherings, the group's dynamics shifted over time, and members have come and gone... Now, just the four of us remain actively involved.

t_ch: 我原本构思过一些活动企划，但因为他们都是树庭的工作人员，闲暇时间经常被用来做各种研究课题，就……
t_en: I had envisioned several activities for us, but since everyone else is employed at the Grove, their free time is usually taken up by all sorts of research projects, so...

t_ch: …唉。命运有时候就是这么蛮不讲理。
t_en: ...Ah, such is unreasonable fate.

t_ch: 聊聊你自己。
t_en: Tell me about yourself.

t_ch: …我？呃，我的事情好像没什么可讲的。
t_en: ...Me? Well, there isn't much to say.

t_ch: 我以前给浴场当过保安，给商队当过护卫，不过现在只能做一些从边远地区为大家寻找失物的委托。
t_en: I used to work security at the baths and served as a guard for caravans, but now my gigs are mostly about retrieving lost items from remote areas for everyone.

t_ch: 这工作听上去有点像所谓的「冒险者」，但其实不过每天在传信石版上跟进树庭发布的各种信息，推测黑潮的活动情况，从中规划一条相对安全的寻物路线……
t_en: While it might sound like the life of an "adventurer," it's mostly about checking on the teleslate information from the Grove, predicting the movements of the black tide, and mapping out a relatively safe route for retrieval missions...

t_ch: …没多少技术含量，算是个看天吃饭的活计吧。
t_en: ...It's not a skilled job, really. Just one that depends on the whims of fate.

t_ch: 没什么想聊的了。
t_en: I have nothing more to chat about.

t_ch: 哦，没关系。最近是多事之秋…您也要多注意安全。
t_en: Oh, that's alright. These are tumultuous times... Take care and stay safe.

t_ch: 抱歉，我只是路过。
t_en: Sorry, I was just passing through.

t_ch: 哦，没关系。最近是多事之秋…您也要多注意安全。
t_en: Oh, that's alright. These are tumultuous times... Take care and stay safe.

t_ch: 「死亡是大自然的常态。不平凡的是我们活着。」
t_en: "Death is nature's norm. What is extraordinary is that we live."

t_ch: 现在想起尼科马库斯说过的这句话…真是五味杂陈。
t_en: When I reflect on this line from Nicomachus now, it stirs a complex mix of emotions...

t_ch: 哎，这位朋友，对小玩意感兴趣不？拉冬城的祭仪烛台，哀地里亚的奶酪块，圣女大人亲手做的玩具，开价合适，全都能卖！
t_en: Hey there, friend! Interested in knick-knacks? I've got ritual candlesticks from Ladon, fine cheese from Aidonia, and toys handmade by the Holy Maiden herself — all at great prices!

t_ch: 不是假货吧？
t_en: They aren't counterfeit, are they?

t_ch: 瞧你说的什么话——我向{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}起誓，宣传什么就是什么！一个字都假不了！再说，我哪有能力搞来假的？
t_en: What an accusation! By {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, I swear what I advertise is what you get! Not a word of falsehood! Besides, how could I possibly procure counterfeits?

t_ch: 哪来这么些东西？
t_en: Where did all these items come from?

t_ch: 当然是靠进货啦！我向{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}起誓，这些全是产地直销，可谓正统的无本实诚买卖！
t_en: It's all about sourcing! By {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, I swear these are all directly from the producers, genuinely honest dealings!

t_ch: 就拿这埃杜利亚的羽笔来说吧，用的可是艾格勒身上掉落的羽毛！那城邦离得多远啊？让大地兽吃撑了全速赶路，都得从纷争月走到机缘月去。
t_en: Take this feather pen from Idilia, for instance. Made from feathers shed by Aquila! That city-state is miles away. Even if a dromas gorged itself and ran at full speed, it would have to leave in the Month of Strife and only get there in the Month of Fortune.

t_ch: 说来也巧，那地方的朝圣者昨天组团来了，带了好多贡品，我看它堆在边上，模样也别致，就悄悄进了货。总共就这么一个，先到先得！
t_en: Funny enough, a group of pilgrims from that place came through yesterday, bringing loads of offerings. One of them caught my eye — it looked pretty unique — so I snagged it for the stock. There's only one, so it's first come, first served!

t_ch: 偷的？举报了。
t_en: Stolen? I'm reporting this.

t_ch: 哎，果然你也不理解。劝你别去揭发，只会让那些卫兵白白收笔贿赂。
t_en: Ah, as expected, you don't understand me either. Trust me. Don't bother reporting it. It'll just end with the guards pocketing bribes for nothing.

t_ch: 我会详细给你解释，就别在那种人身上浪费时间了，用利衡币就能收买的人，跟他们对上眼我都觉得晦气！
t_en: I'll explain the whole thing to you, but don't waste your time on such people. Associating with those who can be bought with Balance Coins is bad luck for me!

t_ch: 卫兵——
t_en: Guard —!

t_ch: 附近的卫兵看了你一眼，却无动于衷。
t_en: A nearby guard glances at you but remains indifferent.

t_ch: 哎，你也不理解。但我会详细解释的，别在那种人身上浪费时间了，用利衡币就能收买的人，跟他们对上眼我都觉得晦气！
t_en: Ah, you don't understand me either. I'll explain the whole thing, but don't waste your time on such people. Dealing with anyone who can be bought off with Balance Coins just feels like inviting bad luck!

t_ch: 不要把我跟那些小偷混为一谈，我是塔兰顿的信徒，贪赃徇私的事我绝不做！再说，我自己也压根不缺钱，根本不会为了生计而偷窃！
t_en: Don't lump me in with those thieves. I am a disciple of Talanton, and I would never engage in corruption! Besides, I'm not short on money, so I wouldn't steal to make ends meet!

t_ch: 但你要知道，如果我不拿，那些贡品的下场会如何？不是被祭司偷偷吞了，就是堆起来全丢进海里。合适吗？不合适啊！
t_en: But think about it — what would happen to those offerings if I didn't take them? They'd either be secretly pocketed by the priests or tossed into the sea. Does that sound right to you? Absolutely not!

t_ch: 身为{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的追随者，我有义务让所有物品的价值得到公正评判。为此，即便要背上偷盗的骂名，我也心甘情愿。
t_en: As a follower of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, it is my solemn duty to ensure every item is assessed with fairness and justice. For this cause, I'm even willing to bear the shame of being called a thief.

t_ch: 难以理解。
t_en: I'm struggling to understand this.

t_ch: 原来你是个大善人？
t_en: So you consider yourself a righteous man?

t_ch: 行了，不必多言，这就是我坚持的律法，问心无愧。
t_en: Nevermind then. This is the law I uphold, and my conscience is clear.

t_ch: 话说回来，你要是不买，我们可以互相行个方便。但如果有感兴趣的，我们就再聊聊？
t_en: Anyway, if you're not buying, maybe we can both excuse ourselves. But if you're interested, we can keep talking.

t_ch: ——密底斯泰歇的图章，放凉的贡果派，全都可卖，全都好价！
t_en: — Mythemis' seals and chilled pies make from offering fruits! Everything's for sale, all at great prices!

t_ch: 嗨，是你啊。怎么样，对做买卖有兴趣了吗？
t_en: Hey, it's you. So, what do you say? Interested in making a deal?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 问吧问吧，正好这会儿没什么生意。
t_en: Go ahead. Business is slow right now.

t_ch: 再说说你的理念？
t_en: Tell me your philosophy again?

t_ch: 「我，达格拉，塔兰顿的忠实信徒，向天秤起誓——我将遵从内心的律法，为物品的价值得到公正对待而永远抗争。此心可鉴，至死…方休！」
t_en: "I, Dagra, a devoted disciple of Talanton, swear upon the scales — I shall fight tirelessly for the fair valuation of all things, abiding by the law within my heart. Let this conviction stand, until death... and beyond!"

t_ch: 话说回来，卫兵们不理解，说这就是偷窃，还要让祭司抓捕我。但他们不也暗地里往自己口袋里塞吗？可笑！
t_en: As for the guards, they just don't get it. They call it theft and want the priests to arrest me. But aren't the priests the same ones secretly stuffing their own pockets? Ridiculous!

t_ch: 听说你还拿过圣女的东西？
t_en: Have you ever taken anything from the Holy Maiden?

t_ch: 唉，实话说，我真心对不起圣女大人。上次进货的时候，误拿了她的手工制品，导致她一直把我当个小偷。但圣女大人的小玩意…不得不说，都是划时代的产物。
t_en: Ah, to tell the truth, I do owe the Holy Maiden an apology. The last time I was stocking up, I accidentally took one of her handmade creations, and she's seen me as a thief ever since. But her trinkets... I've got to admit, they're revolutionary creations!

t_ch: 想想，雅努斯密径的微型版、远距离传信的杯子，甚至还有会动的奇兽模型…我太想让它们展露在世人面前了，所以后来按捺不住心情，又拿了几次。
t_en: Just imagine — a miniaturized version of Janus's Hidden Passage, a cup that enables long-distance communication, and even animated models of wondrous beasts... I couldn't help myself. I was so eager to show these wonders to the world that I ended up taking a few more.

t_ch: 圣女总不会浪费吧。
t_en: The Holy Maiden would never waste anything.

t_ch: 都这样了，你还说自己不是偷？
t_en: Even after all that, you still insist you're not stealing?

t_ch: 别急，或许你也听说了，圣女的物品到最后都是要被祭司没收的，理由是什么「将借来的时间归还给命运」…明眼人都听得懂这是什么意思吧？
t_en: Hold on. Perhaps you've heard that all things belonging to the Holy Maiden are ultimately confiscated by the priests, with the excuse of "returning borrowed time to destiny"... But let's be honest. Anyone with a bit of sense knows what's going on.

t_ch: 要是我不拿，最后不都便宜了那些祭司。圣女能留下什么呢？什么都没有！
t_en: If I don't take them, they'll just end up benefiting those priests. What would the Holy Maiden have left? Nothing!

t_ch: 唉，虔诚信徒所行之路，总是遍布荆棘啊。
t_en: Ah, the path of a faithful disciple is always riddled with thorns.

t_ch: 别偷了，踏实做人吧。
t_en: Quit taking what isn't yours and live an honest life.

t_ch: 哈哈，那是你践行的律法，我可没必要遵从。不过我理解，在这里还能遇见如此坚持正义的人，想必你也会受到塔兰顿的赞扬吧。
t_en: Ha, that's your law to follow, not mine. But I get it. Running into someone so devoted to justice around here is a rare thing. I'm sure Talanton would commend you for it.

t_ch: 回见。愿命运泰坦们庇佑你！
t_en: Farewell. May the Fate Titans bless you!

t_ch: 别偷了，踏实做人吧。
t_en: Quit taking what isn't yours and live an honest life.

t_ch: 哈哈，那是你践行的律法，我可没必要遵从。不过我理解，在这里还能遇见如此坚持正义的人，想必你也会受到塔兰顿的赞扬吧。
t_en: Ha, that's your law to follow, not mine. But I get it. Running into someone so devoted to justice around here is a rare thing. I'm sure Talanton would commend you for it.

t_ch: 回见。愿命运泰坦们庇佑你！
t_en: Farewell. May the Fate Titans bless you!

t_ch: 阁下，冒昧打扰。我曾看到您从神殿内走出来，但只有相关人员才能进入那片区域。看您的打扮不像是普通的朝圣者，烦请告知您的身份？
t_en: Forgive my intrusion, esteemed one. I noticed you emerging from the temple, a place reserved for authorized personnel. Given your attire, you don't appear to be a regular pilgrim. May I ask who you are?

t_ch: 圣女让我来的。
t_en: The Holy Maiden asked for me to be here.

t_ch: 怎会…是圣女大人的客人吗！
t_en: How could it be... are you a guest of the Holy Maiden?

t_ch: 听闻圣女大人深居简出，若是她亲自邀请，想必您也是位行位高权重的祭司！很荣幸能认识您！
t_en: I've heard the Holy Maiden rarely shows herself in public. If she personally invited you, then you must be a priest of considerable rank! It's truly an honor to meet you!

t_ch: 我是未来人。
t_en: I am from the future.

t_ch: 未来…您是在暗示岁月的权能吗？莫非您是一位来自异邦的欧洛尼斯祭司？
t_en: The future... Are you alluding to the power of time? Could it be that you are an Oronyx priest from other cities?

t_ch: 嗯，我确实听说最近会有圣女大人的贵客来访，如此就说得通了！很荣幸能认识您！
t_en: Ah, that would make sense. I recently learned that the Holy Maiden was expecting an esteemed guest. It's truly an honor to meet you!

t_ch: 阁下，虽然我们才刚相识，但我有一个不情之请，还请您原谅…您面见过圣女大人，能否透露下她是位怎样的人？
t_en: Esteemed one, though we've just met, I have a small favor to ask. Please forgive me... You've seen the Holy Maiden, so could you share what she's like?

t_ch: 你没见过她吗？
t_en: Have you not seen her?

t_ch: 很遗憾…我只是个卫兵，并没有踏入内殿的权限……
t_en: Regrettably... I'm just a guard, and I don't have the privilege to enter the inner sanctum...

t_ch: 我了解的也不多…
t_en: I know little about her myself...

t_ch: 这样啊，好吧…我只是个卫兵，从未被允许踏入内殿，所以很好奇……
t_en: I see, well... I'm just a guard, never allowed inside the inner sanctum, so I'm curious...

t_ch: 就算是只言片语也好，请您讲讲吧！
t_en: Even a few words would be appreciated. Please share!

t_ch: 拗不过这位热情的卫兵，你只好简单描述了番记忆中的圣女模样……
t_en: Unable to resist the guard's enthusiasm, you briefly describe the Holy Maiden as you remember her...

t_ch: 啊啊…正如神谕所说，她果然是位善良的人…阁下，还请收下我的谢礼，请将它视作我对命运的进贡……
t_en: Ah... just as the prophecy foretold, she truly is a kind soul. Esteemed one, please accept this token of my gratitude — consider it my humble tribute to destiny.

t_ch: 这我可不能收。
t_en: I'm afraid I can't accept this.

t_ch: 啊…我忘了您来自异邦，和雅努萨波利斯这边的习俗不同。
t_en: Ah... I forgot you're from a foreign city. Customs here in Janusopolis must be unfamiliar to you.

t_ch: 这么大方的吗？
t_en: Such generosity, isn't it?

t_ch: 不会不会，您没有责怪份量太少，已经让我很惶恐了。对…我忘了您来自异邦，习俗不同……
t_en: No, no, the fact that you didn't comment on how little it is has me on edge. Oh... I forgot you're from a foreign city, and our customs must be unfamiliar to you.

t_ch: 多么清廉的大人啊，与圣殿的祭司完全不同…我对您的感激之情无以言表。阁下，若是有要了解的事，请尽管来找我。我必定知无不言！
t_en: Such integrity, esteemed one, so unlike the temple's priests... I'm deeply grateful. Noble one, if there's anything you wish to know, please feel free to inquire. I'll share everything I can!

t_ch: 异邦的祭司阁下，很高兴再见到您！您今日的气色也很好，想必是命运眷顾！有什么是我能帮到您的？
t_en: Priest from the outlands, it's good to see you again! You look great today. Destiny must favor you! How may I assist you?

t_ch: 来聊聊吧。
t_en: Let's chat.

t_ch: 是！阁下，请讲！
t_en: Yes, esteemed one, please speak!

t_ch: 你对泰坦信仰怎么看？
t_en: What is your view on the Titan faith?

t_ch: 不不，像我这种卫兵，怎敢对祭祀这种大事妄加议论啊！实在是诚惶诚恐……
t_en: No, no, as a mere guard, how could I dare to comment on something as sacred as a ritual? The very thought makes me uneasy...

t_ch: 请您放心，祭司们的教诲我会铭记——{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}为我们指引前路，而{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的教典正是卫士的根基！
t_en: Rest assured, I heed the teachings of the priests. — {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} guides our path, and the scriptures of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} are the foundation of every guard!

t_ch: 相比这个，不如您再跟我多讲讲圣女大人的事吧！她是位怎样的人？是否就像神谕说的那样，模样优雅，话语足以震慑人心？
t_en: Rather than that, tell me more about the Holy Maiden! What is she like? Is she truly as graceful and commanding as the prophecy foretold?

t_ch: 考虑到这里是「过去」的空间，稍微透露些应该也不要紧，但你并不了解他所熟知的那位圣女，那么……
t_en: Considering this is the "past," revealing a little should be fine, but you don't know which Holy Maiden he's referring to, so...

t_ch: （讲讲缇宝吧）
t_en: (Tell him about Tribbie)

t_ch: 你描述了缇宝给你的印象，除了身高的部分……
t_en: You describe your impression of Tribbie, carefully leaving out any mention of her height...

t_ch: 哦哦，热情烂漫！正符合神谕的描述，与前代圣女如出一辙，多么令人欣喜的传承啊！
t_en: Oh, so spirited and full of life! She's exactly as the prophecy foretold, just like the previous Holy Maiden. What a wonderful legacy!

t_ch: （讲讲缇宁吧）
t_en: (Talk about Trinnon)

t_ch: 你描述了缇宁给你的印象，除了身高的部分……
t_en: You describe your impression of Trinnon, carefully leaving out any mention of her height...

t_ch: 哦哦，冷静而知性的少女，她尽责的信念实在让人感动…多么伟大的人物啊！
t_en: Oh, such a calm and intelligent young woman. Her dedication to duty is truly inspiring... What a remarkable individual!

t_ch: （讲讲缇安吧）
t_en: (Talk about Trianne)

t_ch: 你描述了缇安给你的印象，除了身高的部分……
t_en: You describe your impression of Trianne, carefully leaving out any mention of her height...

t_ch: 哦哦，活泼顽皮的圣女！即便受到命运眷顾，仍旧保留着心中那份纯真，多么难能可贵啊！
t_en: Oh, a lively and spirited Holy Maiden! Even with destiny's favor, she still retains her innocence. How rare and precious!

t_ch: 感谢您，祭司阁下！阁下的话语令我蒙受神恩，我可真是太幸运了！
t_en: Thank you, Honorable Priest! Your words are a blessing to me. I truly am fortunate.

t_ch: 你很虔诚嘛。
t_en: You are indeed faithful.

t_ch: …啊，能得到阁下的认同，实在不胜荣幸…感激涕零…嘶嘶。
t_en: ...Oh, to receive your acknowledgment is the greatest honor... I am deeply grateful.

t_ch: 不瞒您说，我在奥莱诺斯的老家一直都维持着坚定的信仰，对泰坦的捐赠额度也是全城数一数二。家人从小就教我祈祷的流程，希望我能为信仰献身。
t_en: In my hometown of Oleinus, I've always been deeply devoted to my faith. My contributions to the Titans are among the highest in the city. From a young age, my family taught me the sacred rituals of prayer, instilling in me a hope to one day dedicate my life fully to the faith.

t_ch: 如今，我如愿来到雅努萨波利斯，但却被告知身份不够，无法进入圣殿。幸蒙父母赐我的健硕身躯，得以成为卫士，供奉薪饷的七成给泰坦，继续践行我的信仰。
t_en: Now, I have come to Janusopolis as I wished, but was told I am not eligible to enter the sacred temple. Fortunately, my robust physique allowed me to become a guard, dedicate seventy percent of my salary to the Titans, and continue the practice of my faith.

t_ch: 有同僚嘲笑我的行为，说祭司们有私心，我这样的行为毫无意义。但是…「虔诚的信念，比起对错更为重要。」这是我的家人教我的第一课，我对此无比感激。
t_en: Some of my peers make fun of me, saying the priests are self-serving and that what I'm doing is pointless. But my family always taught me that "faith is about devotion, not about being right or wrong," and I'm truly grateful for that lesson.

t_ch: 有其余见到圣女的办法吗？
t_en: Is there another way to see the Holy Maiden?

t_ch: 阁下，您的意思莫非是…按照规定，动用雅努斯迷津进行卜算，需要有卫士陪同，这样…就能带我在不违背律法的前提下进入内殿？
t_en: Esteemed one, are you suggesting...? According to the rules, using Janus' Maze for divination requires a guard's presence, which... would allow me to enter the inner sanctum without breaking the law?

t_ch: 我还什么都没说呢。
t_en: I haven't said anything yet.

t_ch: 早有预谋是吧？
t_en: So you've been planning this, eh?

t_ch: 让您见笑了，这是我在脑中模拟了千百遍的情形，路线图我早就规划好了。阁下，事不宜迟！我们……
t_en: You caught me. This is a scenario I've rehearsed in my mind a thousand times, and I've planned the route in detail. Esteemed one, there's no time to waste! We should...

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: What's the matter?

t_ch: 别犹豫啊！
t_en: Don't hesitate!

t_ch: …还是算了吧。辜负了阁下好意…请恕罪，我绝没有消遣的意思，只是刚才那瞬间，我回想起了神谕。
t_en: ...Forget it. I shouldn't have misinterpreted your kindness... Please forgive me. I had no intention of being disrespectful. It's just that... at that moment... I recalled the prophecy.

t_ch: 「为使门径璨璨生辉，继任者将息心隐居，免受叨扰」——那是前前前代圣女踏上朝圣之旅后的第一条神谕，数百个光历年前，由命运三相殿的祭司代为传达。
t_en: "To keep the passage radiant, the successor must live in seclusion, undisturbed." This was the first prophecy issued centuries ago by the Holy Maiden countless generations ago, conveyed through the priests of the Temple of the Three Fates hundreds of Light Calendar years ago.

t_ch: 曾听闻圣女大人和蔼亲切，而能进入圣殿的，多半都是贡献良多的虔诚信徒，我想所谓的「叨扰」，指的可能就是我这样不自知的家伙吧。
t_en: I've heard the Holy Maiden is approachable and kind, and those allowed into the temple are often faithful disciples. Perhaps the "disturbance" refers to unaware individuals like myself.

t_ch: 有您对圣女大人的描述，已经足够滋养我的信念了！请放心，我会全力以赴，保卫雅努萨波利斯远离一切威胁！
t_en: Your words about the Holy Maiden are more than enough to inspire my faith! Rest assured, I will do everything to protect Janusopolis from all threats!

t_ch: 不打扰你了，再会啦。
t_en: I won't bother you anymore, farewell.

t_ch: 是！您慢走，祭司大人。愿圣女的慈爱永远与您，还有我同在！
t_en: Yes! Safe travels, noble priest. May the Holy Maiden's mercy be with you, and with me as well!

t_ch: 不打扰你了，再会啦。
t_en: I won't bother you anymore, farewell.

t_ch: 是！您慢走，祭司大人。愿圣女的慈爱永远与您，还有我同在！
t_en: Yes! Safe travels, noble priest. May the Holy Maiden's mercy be with you, and with me as well!

t_ch: 最近对你的印象有改变！
t_en: Lately, my impression of you has changed.

t_ch: 咦，是吗？方便问一问阁下，是在何时、因何事，产生了什么样的印象变化呢？
t_en: Oh? Really? If I may ask, when and because of what did your impression change?

t_ch: 一起去树庭的时候。
t_en: When we went to the Grove together.

t_ch: 的确，相伴旅行能让人发现朋友身上不为人知的一面。即使是相识已久的缇安老师，在这次树庭之行里，同样露出些隐蔽的性情。
t_en: Indeed, traveling together often reveals sides of a friend that were previously unseen. Even Trianne, whom I've known for so long, showed hidden sides of her personality on this trip to the Grove.

t_ch: 至于阁下…也让我看到此前并不了解的一面。例如，阁下虽然和缇安老师一样洒脱纯粹，可关键时刻又很靠得住呢。
t_en: As for you... you, too, showed me a side I didn't know. For example, while you're as carefree and pure as Trianne, you're also incredibly reliable in critical moments.

t_ch: 诶？问我对阁下的初印象吗？说来可笑，当时看到阁下与白厄站在一块儿，我还以为奥赫玛又迎来一位板正的救世主了呢。
t_en: Oh? What was my first impression of you? Funny story — when I first saw you standing with Phainon, I thought Okhema had welcomed yet another upright Deliverer.

t_ch: 你很会照顾人们的心情。
t_en: You're good at taking care of people's emotions.

t_ch: 啊，谢谢夸奖…这大概要多谢我与死亡打了千年的交道，作为刽子手，对将死之人的心理已经训练出极为细腻的揣度……
t_en: Ah, thank you for the compliment... I suppose I have to thank the thousand years I've spent brushing shoulders with death. As an executioner, I've trained myself to deeply understand the minds of those nearing their end...

t_ch: …阁下怎么沉默了？…地狱笑话又是什么意思？
t_en: Why are you silent? And... what does this phrase "black comedy" mean...?

t_ch: 比初印象要有趣、可亲得多。
t_en: You're much more approachable and interesting than my first impression of you.

t_ch: 啊…阁下愿意这么说，我很开心。我平素深居简出，又身负死亡的诅咒，已经很久没有认识新的朋友了，也许给人的初印象确实有些冰冷……
t_en: Ah, I'm glad you think so. I've spent much of my life in seclusion, bearing the curse of death, so it's been a long time since I made new friends. Perhaps my initial impression does come across as cold.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人曾嘱咐我待人更加亲切温柔些。可人们对我不是退避三舍就是恭敬有加，似乎这份隔阂并不那么容易消弭吧。
t_en: Lady Aglaea once advised me to be gentler and more amiable in my demeanor. But people either avoid me altogether or treat me with great reverence — this distance is not so easily bridged.

t_ch: 那么，我就把阁下的这番话作为勉励吧。希望在不远的将来，阁下能在我身上看到更加可喜的改变。
t_en: Let me take your words as encouragement, then. I hope that in the near future, you'll see even more welcoming changes in me.

t_ch: 你真的要独自返乡、抵御黑潮？
t_en: Are you really going to return home alone and face the black tide?

t_ch: 记忆的残像似乎晃了晃，过往回忆中的言语，因其承载的情感而重现……
t_en: The fragment of memory seems to waver — words once spoken are reappearing now due to the emotion they contain...

t_ch: …假如万事顺利，我们也许不会再见。感谢你们为翁法罗斯带来的一切。
t_en: If all goes well, we may not meet again. Thank you for everything you've brought to Amphoreus.

t_ch: 多锻炼，少熬夜。
t_en: Train more, stay up late less.

t_ch: …呵，真是鼓舞人心的话语。
t_en: ...Haha, such inspiring words.

t_ch: 男孩子独自出门，保护好自己。
t_en: Stay safe out there. A lone boy can get into a lot of trouble.

t_ch: …呵，真是感人至深的道别辞。
t_en: ...Haha, such a heartfelt farewell.

t_ch: 没了你我们可怎么办，呜呜呜。
t_en: *sob* ...What are we going to do without you?

t_ch: …呵，开拓者，又开始说胡话了么？
t_en: ...Haha, Trailblazer, are you rambling again?

t_ch: 好像没什么想聊的了。
t_en: I don't think I have more to chat about.

t_ch: 言尽于此。再会。
t_en: That is all I have to say. Farewell.

t_ch: 如今我身负神权，隐隐能感觉到…你们对翁法罗斯的未来重要非凡。
t_en: Now that I bear the divine within me, I can get the vague feeling that... that you have a great significance for the future of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 此去恐无归期。我能否将后背交给你们，恳请你们…继续协助逐火的征程？
t_en: With this departure, I fear that I will never again return. Can I entrust you to watch my back... and to continue supporting the Flame-Chase Journey in my absence?

t_ch: 对黄金裔们的评价？
t_en: What do you think of the Chrysos Heirs?

t_ch: 纷争世以来的漫长岁月，有无数世人、无数眷属、无数黄金裔与我、与奥赫玛、与泰坦们产生了命运的交织。不知你要问哪些人？
t_en: Over the many long years since the Era Bellica, there have been countless people, Titankins, and Chrysos Heirs who have formed bonds of destiny with myself, Okhema, and the Titans. Which ones are you referring to?

t_ch: 纷争之新神…万敌！
t_en: The new god of Strife... Mydei!

t_ch: 决然接下「天谴之矛」的火种，以不容置喙的王令终结悬锋王朝，又为族人带来荣耀的崭新未来……
t_en: He decided to accept the Coreflame of the Lance of Fury, ended the dynasty of Kremnos with an unquestionable royal decree, and brought his people a new future of glory...

t_ch: 说来有趣，近来我在悬锋人的闲谈里，已经能听见「愿迈德漠斯捍卫你」这般的祝愿了。
t_en: Interestingly, the phrase "May Mydeimos protect you" has started to appear in the conversations of Kremnoans.

t_ch: 如今孤身把守悬锋、将黑潮尽数拦截的迈德漠斯，在众人心目中，已然当得起翁法罗斯守护者一称。
t_en: Now remaining alone at Castrum Kremnos and holding back the entirety of the black tide, Mydeimos has undoubtedly earned the title of Guardian of Amphoreus in the hearts of the people.

t_ch: 缇宝、缇宁，以及…缇安。
t_en: Tribbie, Trinnon, and... Trianne.

t_ch: …不知在送别缇安后，阁下对吾师的印象是否有所转变。
t_en: ...After you bid farewell to Trianne, has your impression of my teacher changed?

t_ch: 她们并非如外表看上去的那般无忧无虑…在缇安之前，无情的命运已将千万次同样悲伤的离别交予她们，而吾师的步伐却从未犹疑。
t_en: They are not as carefree as they appear... Before Trianne's loss, merciless fate had already brought them countless similarly sad partings, yet my teacher's steps never faltered.

t_ch: …愿我们都能如吾师所期盼的那般，与诸多逝者在那鲜花芬芳的西风彼岸重逢。
t_en: ...May we all, as my teacher hopes, reunite with the many departed at the end of the west wind where flowers bloom in spring.

t_ch: 身世神秘的遐蝶。
t_en: Castorice, the one with a mysterious backstory.

t_ch: 与她相处的这些时日，想必你已发觉，遐蝶比绝大多数人要更加热爱生活、珍视生命。这样的人却身负死亡的诅咒，不能不说是命运的玩笑。
t_en: Now that you've spent time with her, you must have noticed that Castorice cherishes life and loves living far more than most. For someone like her to bear the curse of death — it's nothing short of a cruel joke by fate.

t_ch: 至于她的身世…不如直接找她当面询问吧？呵…她可不是个擅长拒绝的孩子啊。
t_en: As for her backstory... why not ask her directly? Heh... She's not the kind to easily refuse others.

t_ch: 其他黄金裔。
t_en: Other Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 与我十分甚至九分像的白厄。
t_en: My almost-doppelgänger, Phainon.

t_ch: …无缺的英雄。时至今日，我仍会如此评价白厄。作为最完美的黄金裔，他注定在逐火之旅中背负沉重的救世使命。
t_en: ...The perfect hero. To this day, that is still the opinion I have of Phainon. As the most perfect Chrysos Heir, he is fated to bear the heavy fate of saving the world in the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 尽管他未能承载尼卡多利的火种…但这并不意味着他有失资质。
t_en: Although he could not bear Nikador's Coreflame... that does not mean he lacks the qualifications.

t_ch: 属于他的命运，将在不远的未来，如同烈阳一般冉冉升起。
t_en: His destiny will rise like the blazing sun in the near future.

t_ch: 你们的大表演家，那刻夏。
t_en: You lots' Great Performer, Anaxa.

t_ch: 愚钝的阿那克萨戈拉斯，树庭七贤人之一，从事渎神的「炼金术」研究，行事狂狷不羁、独爱口放厥词。
t_en: Anaxagoras the fool, one of the Seven Sages of the Grove, who engages in heretical alchemical research and acts with reckless arrogance, loving to spew nonsense with abandon.

t_ch: 「金织」与智种学派贤人的宿怨无需隐瞒，但我向你陈述的并非偏见，而是坊间对他最广为流传的画像。而他真正的面相，要由你自己判断。
t_en: The grudge between the Goldweaver and the Sage of Nousporism needs no concealment. However, what I've described is not prejudice, but rather the image of him most widely known in the public eye. His true nature? That's for you to judge.

t_ch: 笑容很甜的风堇。
t_en: Hyacine, whose smile is really sweet.

t_ch: 上次与你介绍风堇时，我提到她是「昏光庭院」的首席护理师。但事实上，她也是这个组织的继承人与管理者。
t_en: The last time I introduced Hyacine to you, I mentioned she is the chief healer of the Twilight Courtyard. But in truth, she's also the heir and manager of this organization.

t_ch: 「昏光庭院」存世已久，只是因天空一族式微而逐渐凋亡。风堇让这个名号再临世间，以微光与温柔疗愈众人…想必也背负着殷切的梦想吧。
t_en: The Twilight Courtyard has existed for a long time, though it faded into obscurity with the decline of the Sky people. Hyacine has brought this name back into the world, healing others with her gentleness and light... She must be carrying her own deep dreams and aspirations.

t_ch: 都是熟人了，不问了。
t_en: I know everyone well, and there's nothing more I need to know.

t_ch: 都是熟人了，不问了。
t_en: I know everyone well, and there's nothing more I need to know.

t_ch: 无论哪位黄金裔，都是此世不可或缺的英杰。我其实也无权代替这个时代评判他们，因为他们都承载着这个世界的未来。
t_en: Every member of the Chrysos Heirs is a hero who this world cannot do without. Even I have no right to cast appraisal on them on behalf of this era, because they all shoulder the future of this world.
